G4355.meaning: <meaning><def>to take to oneself</def>, i.e. use (food), lead (aside), admit (to friendship or hospitality)</meaning>

G4357.meaning: <meaning><def>to stay further</def>, i.e. remain in a place, with a person; figuratively, to adhere to, persevere in</meaning>
G4357.usage: <usage>abide still, be with, cleave unto, continue in (with).</usage>

G4358.source: <source>from G4314 (πρός) and a derivative of the same as G3730 (ὁρμή) (meaning to tie (anchor) or lull);</source>
G4358.meaning: <meaning><def>to moor to</def>, i.e. (by implication) land at</meaning>

G4360.source: <source>from G4314 (πρός) and a form of (to be vexed with something irksome);</source>

G4361.source: <source>from G4314 (πρός) and the same as G3983 (πεινάω);</source>

G4362.meaning: <meaning><def>to fasten to</def>, i.e. (specially), to impale (on a cross)</meaning>

G4363.meaning: <meaning><def>to fall towards</def>, i.e. (gently) prostrate oneself (in supplication or homage), or (violently) to rush upon (in storm)</meaning>

G4364.meaning: <meaning><def>to do forward for oneself</def>, i.e. pretend (as if about to do a thing)</meaning>

G4365.meaning: <meaning><def>to journey towards</def>, i.e. approach (not the same as G4313 (προπορεύομαι))</meaning>

G4366.meaning: <meaning><def>to tear towards</def>, i.e. burst upon (as a tempest or flood)</meaning>

G4369.meaning: <meaning><def>to place additionally</def>, i.e. lay beside, annex, repeat</meaning>
G4369.usage: <usage>add, again, give more, increase, lay unto, proceed further, speak to any more.</usage>

G4370.meaning: <meaning><def>to run towards</def>, i.e. hasten to meet or join</meaning>

G4371.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of a compound of G4314 (πρός) and G5315 (φάγω);</source>
G4371.meaning: <meaning><def>something eaten in addition to bread</def>, i.e. a relish (specially, fish; compare G3795 (ὀψάριον))</meaning>

G4372.source: <source>from G4253 (πρό) and a derivative of G4969 (σφάζω);</source>

G4374.meaning: <meaning><def>to bear towards</def>, i.e. lead to, tender (especially to God), treat</meaning>
G4374.usage: <usage>bring (to, unto), deal with, do, offer (unto, up), present unto, put to.</usage>

G4375.source: <source>from a presumed compound of G4314 (πρός) and G5368 (φιλέω);</source>

G4377.meaning: <meaning><def>to sound towards</def>, i.e. address, exclaim, summon</meaning>

G4378.source: <source>from a comparative of G4314 (πρός) and (to pour);</source>

G4379.meaning: <meaning><def>to impinge</def>, i.e. lay a finger on (in order to relieve)</meaning>

G4380.meaning: <meaning><def>to favor an individual</def>, i.e. show partiality</meaning>

G4381.meaning: <meaning><def>an accepter of a face</def> (individual), i.e. (specially), one exhibiting partiality</meaning>

G4383.meaning: <meaning><def>the front</def> (as being towards view), i.e. the countenance, aspect, appearance, surface; by implication, presence, person</meaning>
G4383.usage: <usage>(outward) appearance, X before, countenance, face, fashion, (men's) person, presence.</usage>

G4386.source: <source>neuter of G4387 (πρότερος) as adverb (with or without the article);</source>

G4388.meaning: <meaning><def>to place before</def>, i.e. (for oneself) to exhibit; (to oneself) to propose (determine)</meaning>

G4389.source: <source>middle voice from G4253 (πρό) and the base of G5157 (τροπή);</source>
G4389.meaning: <meaning><def>to turn forward for oneself</def>, i.e. encourage</meaning>

G4391.meaning: <meaning><def>to exist before</def>, i.e. (adverbially) to be or do something previously</meaning>

G4392.source: <source>from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G5316 (φαίνω);</source>

G4395.meaning: <meaning><def>to foretell events</def>, divine, speak under inspiration, exercise the prophetic office</meaning>

G4396.source: <source>from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G5346 (φημί);</source>
G4396.meaning: <meaning><def>a foreteller</def> ("prophet"); by analogy, an inspired speaker; by extension, a poet</meaning>

G4398.meaning: <meaning><def>a female foreteller or an inspired woman</def></meaning>

G4399.meaning: <meaning><def>to get an earlier start of</def>, i.e. anticipate</meaning>

G4400.source: <source>middle voice from G4253 (πρό) and a derivative of G5495 (χείρ);</source>
G4400.meaning: <meaning><def>to handle for oneself in advance</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to purpose</meaning>

G4404.meaning: <meaning><def>at dawn</def>; by implication, the day-break watch</meaning>

G4405.source: <source>feminine of a derivative of G4404 (πρωΐ) as noun;</source>

G4406.meaning: <meaning><def>dawning</def>, i.e. (by analogy) autumnal (showering, the first of the rainy season)</meaning>

G4408.source: <source>feminine of a presumed derivative of G4253 (πρό) as noun;</source>
G4408.meaning: <meaning><def>the prow</def>, i.e. forward part of a vessel</meaning>

G4410.meaning: <meaning><def>a sitting first</def> (in the front row), i.e. preeminence in council</meaning>

G4411.meaning: <meaning><def>a reclining first</def> (in the place of honor) at the dinner-bed, i.e. preeminence at meals</meaning>

G4412.source: <source>neuter of G4413 (πρῶτος) as adverb (with or without G3588 (ὁ));</source>
G4412.usage: <usage>before, at the beginning, chiefly (at, at the) first (of all).</usage>

G4413.usage: <usage>before, beginning, best, chief(-est), first (of all), former.</usage>

G4414.meaning: <meaning><def>one standing first in the ranks</def>, i.e. a captain (champion)</meaning>

G4416.source: <source>from G4413 (πρῶτος) and the alternate of G5088 (τίκτω);</source>

G4417.meaning: <meaning><def>to trip</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to err, sin, fail (of salvation)</meaning>

G4419.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of G4420 (πτέρυξ);</source>

G4420.source: <source>from a derivative of G4072 (πέτομαι) (meaning a feather);</source>

G4422.source: <source>probably akin to the alternate of G4098 (πίπτω) (through the idea of causing to fall) or to G4072 (πέτομαι) (through that of causing to fly away);</source>

G4426.source: <source>from a presumed derivative of G4429 (πτύω) (and thus akin to G4422 (πτοέω));</source>

G4428.meaning: <meaning><def>and thus apparently allied to G4072</def> (πέτομαι) through the idea of expansion, and to 4429 through that of flattening; compare G3961 (πατέω)); to fold, i.e. furl a scroll</meaning>

G4433.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a beggar</def>, i.e. (by implication) to become indigent (figuratively)</meaning>

G4434.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to G4422</def> (πτοέω) and the alternate of G4098 (πίπτω)); a beggar (as cringing), i.e. pauper (strictly denoting absolute or public mendicancy, although also used in a qualified or relative sense; whereas G3993 (πένης) properly means only straitened circumstances in private), literally (often as noun) or figuratively (distressed)</meaning>

G4435.source: <source>from a primary (the fist as a weapon);</source>
G4435.meaning: <meaning><def>the clenched hand</def>, i.e. (only in dative case as adverb) with the fist (hard scrubbing)</meaning>

G4436.source: <source>from (the name of the region where Delphi, the seat of the famous oracle, was located);</source>
G4436.meaning: <meaning><def>a Python</def>, i.e. (by analogy, with the supposed diviner there) inspiration (soothsaying)</meaning>

G4437.meaning: <meaning><def>clasped</def> (thick), i.e. (figuratively) frequent; neuter plural (as adverb) frequently</meaning>

G4438.source: <source>from a derivative of the same as G4435 (πυγμή);</source>
G4438.meaning: <meaning><def>to box</def> (with the fist), i.e. contend (as a boxer) at the games (figuratively)</meaning>

G4439.meaning: <meaning><def>a gate</def>, i.e. the leaf or wing of a folding entrance (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G4440.meaning: <meaning><def>a gate-way</def>, door-way of a building or city; by implication, a portal or vestibule</meaning>

G4441.source: <source>middle voice prolonged from a primary (which occurs only as an alternate in certain tenses);</source>
G4441.meaning: <meaning><def>to question</def>, i.e. ascertain by inquiry (as a matter of information merely; and thus differing from G2065 (ἐρωτάω), which properly means a request as a favor; and from G154 (αἰτέω), which is strictly a demand for something due; as well as from G2212 (ζητέω), which implies a search for something hidden; and from G1189 (δέομαι), which involves the idea of urgent need); by implication, to learn (by casual intelligence)</meaning>

G4445.meaning: <meaning><def>to be on fire</def>, i.e. (specially), to have a fever</meaning>

G4448.meaning: <meaning><def>to kindle</def>, i.e. (passively) to be ignited, glow (literally), be refined (by implication), or (figuratively) to be inflamed (with anger, grief, lust)</meaning>

G4451.meaning: <meaning><def>ignition</def>, i.e. (specially), smelting (figuratively, conflagration, calamity as a test)</meaning>

G4452.source: <source>another form of the base of G4458 (-πώς);</source>
G4452.meaning: <meaning><def>an enclitic particle of indefiniteness</def>; yet, even; used only in the comparative. See G3369 (μηδέπω), G3380 (μήπω), G3764 (οὐδέπω), G3768 (οὔπω), G4455 (πώποτε).</meaning>

G4453.source: <source>probably ultimately from (to be busy, to trade);</source>
G4453.meaning: <meaning><def>to barter</def> (as a pedlar), i.e. to sell</meaning>

G4455.meaning: <meaning><def>at any time</def>, i.e. (with negative particle) at no time</meaning>
G4455.usage: <usage>at any time, + never (…to any man), + yet, never man.</usage>

G4456.meaning: <meaning><def>to petrify</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to indurate (render stupid or callous)</meaning>

G4458.meaning: <meaning><def>an enclitic particle of indefiniteness of manner</def>; somehow or anyhow; used only in composition</meaning>

G4459.meaning: <meaning><def>an interrogative particle of manner</def>; in what way? (sometimes the question is indirect, how?); also as exclamation, how much!</meaning>

G4461.meaning: <meaning><def>my master</def>, i.e Rabbi, as an official title of honor</meaning>

G4463.meaning: <meaning><def>to strike with a stick</def>, i.e. bastinado</meaning>

G4464.meaning: <meaning><def>a stick or wand</def> (as a cudgel, a cane or a baton of royalty)</meaning>

G4465.meaning: <meaning><def>a rod-</def> (the Latin fasces) holder, i.e. a Roman lictor (constable or executioner)</meaning>

G4467.source: <source>from a comparative of (easy, i.e. reckless) and G2041 (ἔργον);</source>

G4469.meaning: <meaning><def>O empty one</def>, i.e. thou worthless (as a term of utter vilification)</meaning>

G4472.meaning: <meaning><def>to render besprinkled</def>, i.e. asperse (ceremonially or figuratively)</meaning>

G4474.source: <source>from a derivative of a primary (to let fall, "rap");</source>

G4475.usage: <usage>(+ strike with the) palm of the hand, smite with the hand.</usage>

G4476.meaning: <meaning><def>perhaps rather akin to the base of G4474</def> (ῥαπίζω) through the idea of puncturing); a needle</meaning>

G4480.meaning: <meaning><def>a rheda</def>, i.e. four-wheeled carriage (wagon for riding)</meaning>

G4481.source: <source>by incorrect transliteration for a word of Hebrew origin (H03594);</source>

G4482.meaning: <meaning><def>for some tenses of which a prolonged form is used to flow</def> ("run"; as water)</meaning>

G4483.source: <source>perhaps akin (or identical) with G4482 (ῥέω) (through the idea of pouring forth);</source>

G4485.meaning: <meaning><def>something torn</def>, i.e. a fragment (by implication and abstractly, a fall)</meaning>

G4486.source: <source>both prolonged forms of (which appears only in certain forms, and is itself probably a strengthened form of (see in G2608 (κατάγνυμι))) to "break," "wreck" or "crack", i.e. (especially) to sunder (by separation of the parts;</source>
G4486.meaning: <meaning><def>G2608</def> (κατάγνυμι) being its intensive (with the preposition in composition), and G2352 (θραύω) a shattering to minute fragments; but not a reduction to the constituent particles, like G3089 (λύω)) or disrupt, lacerate; by implication, to convulse (with spasms); figuratively, to give vent to joyful emotions</meaning>

G4487.meaning: <meaning><def>an utterance</def> (individually, collectively or specially),; by implication, a matter or topic (especially of narration, command or dispute); with a negative naught whatever</meaning>

G4489.meaning: <meaning><def>a speaker</def>, i.e. (by implication) a forensic advocate</meaning>

G4493.meaning: <meaning><def>a jerk</def> (of the eye, i.e. (by analogy) an instant)</meaning>

G4494.source: <source>from a derivative of G4496 (ῥίπτω) (meaning a fan or bellows);</source>
G4494.meaning: <meaning><def>to breeze up</def>, i.e. (by analogy) to agitate (into waves)</meaning>

G4496.source: <source>a primary verb (perhaps rather akin to the base of G4474 (ῥαπίζω), through the idea of sudden motion);</source>
G4496.meaning: <meaning><def>to fling</def> (properly, with a quick toss, thus differing from G906 (βάλλω), which denotes a deliberate hurl; and from (see in G1614 (ἐκτείνω)), which indicates an extended projection); by qualification, to deposit (as if a load); by extension, to disperse</meaning>

G4501.meaning: <meaning><def>a sabre</def>, i.e. a long and broad cutlass (any weapon of the kind, literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G4505.source: <source>prolongation from G4506 (ῥύομαι) in its original sense;</source>

G4506.source: <source>middle voice of an obsolete verb, akin to G4482 (ῥέω) (through the idea of a current;</source>
G4506.meaning: <meaning><def>compare G4511</def> (ῥύσις)); to rush or draw (for oneself), i.e. rescue</meaning>

G4508.meaning: <meaning><def>dirty</def>, i.e. (relatively) cheap or shabby; morally, wicked</meaning>

G4511.source: <source>from G4506 (ῥύομαι) in the sense of its congener G4482 (ῥέω);</source>

G4512.meaning: <meaning><def>a fold</def> (as drawing together), i.e. a wrinkle (especially on the face)</meaning>

G4515.source: <source>adverb from a presumed derivative of G4516 (Ῥώμη);</source>

G4517.meaning: <meaning><def>probably akin to G4506</def> (ῥύομαι)); to strengthen, i.e. (impersonal passive) have health (as a parting exclamation, good-bye)</meaning>

G4518.meaning: <meaning><def>thou hast left me</def>; sabachthani (i.e. shebakthani), a cry of distress</meaning>

G4519.meaning: <meaning><def>armies</def>; sabaoth (i.e. tsebaoth), a military epithet of God</meaning>

G4520.meaning: <meaning><def>a "sabbatism"</def>, i.e. (figuratively) the repose of Christianity (as a type of heaven)</meaning>

G4521.meaning: <meaning><def>the Sabbath</def> (i.e. Shabbath), or day of weekly repose from secular avocations (also the observance or institution itself); by extension, a se'nnight, i.e. the interval between two Sabbaths; likewise the plural in all the above applications</meaning>

G4522.source: <source>from a derivative of (to equip) meaning furniture, especially a pack-saddle (which in the East is merely a bag of netted rope);</source>

G4523.meaning: <meaning><def>a Sadducæan</def> (i.e. Tsadokian), or follower of a certain heretical Israelite</meaning>

G4525.meaning: <meaning><def>to wag</def> (as a dog its tail fawningly), i.e. (generally) to shake (figuratively, disturb)</meaning>

G4526.meaning: <meaning><def>"sack"-cloth</def>, i.e. mohair (the material or garments made of it, worn as a sign of grief)</meaning>

G4529.source: <source>probably from G4535 (σάλος) (from the surge on the shore);</source>

G4531.meaning: <meaning><def>to waver</def>, i.e. agitate, rock, topple or (by implication) destroy; figuratively, to disturb, incite</meaning>
G4531.usage: <usage>move, shake (together), which can(-not) be shaken, stir up.</usage>

G4536.source: <source>perhaps from G4535 (σάλος) (through the idea of quavering or reverberation);</source>

G4537.meaning: <meaning><def>to trumpet</def>, i.e. sound a blast (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G4540.meaning: <meaning><def>Samaria</def> (i.e. Shomeron), a city and region of Palestine</meaning>

G4543.meaning: <meaning><def>Samo-thrace</def> (Samos of Thrace), an island in the Mediterranean</meaning>

G4547.meaning: <meaning><def>of uncertain origin)</def>; a slipper or sole-pad</meaning>

G4549.meaning: <meaning><def>Saul</def> (i.e. Shaul), the Jewish name of Paul</meaning>

G4555.meaning: <meaning><def>sardine</def> (G3037 (λίθος) being implied), i.e. a gem, so called</meaning>

G4556.source: <source>properly, an adjective from an uncertain base;</source>
G4556.meaning: <meaning><def>sardian</def> (3037 being implied), i.e. (as noun) the gem so called</meaning>

G4557.source: <source>from the base of G4556 (σάρδιος) and (the nail of a finger;</source>
G4557.meaning: <meaning><def>hence the "onyx" stone)</def>; a "sardonyx", i.e. the gem so called</meaning>

G4559.meaning: <meaning><def>pertaining to flesh</def>, i.e. (by extension) bodily, temporal, or (by implication) animal, unregenerate</meaning>

G4561.meaning: <meaning><def>flesh</def> (as stripped of the skin), i.e. (strictly) the meat of an animal (as food), or (by extension) the body (as opposed to the soul (or spirit), or as the symbol of what is external, or as the means of kindred), or (by implication) human nature (with its frailties (physically or morally) and passions), or (specially), a human being (as such)</meaning>

G4563.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to G4951</def> (σύρω)); meaning a broom; to sweep</meaning>

G4567.source: <source>of Chaldee origin corresponding to H04566 (with the definite affix);</source>

G4569.source: <source>of Hebrew origin, the same as G4549 (Σαούλ);</source>
G4569.meaning: <meaning><def>Saulus</def> (i.e. Shaul), the Jewish name of Paul</meaning>

G4570.source: <source>a prolonged form of an apparently primary verb;</source>

G4572.source: <source>genitive case from G4571 (σέ) and G846 (αὐτός), also dative case of the same, , and accusative case , likewise contracted , , and , respectively;</source>

G4573.source: <source>middle voice from a derivative of G4576 (σέβομαι);</source>

G4574.meaning: <meaning><def>something adored</def>, i.e. an object of worship (god, altar, etc)</meaning>

G4575.meaning: <meaning><def>venerable</def> (august), i.e. (as noun) a title of the Roman Emperor, or (as adjective) imperial</meaning>

G4576.source: <source>middle voice of an apparently primary verb;</source>

G4577.source: <source>probably from G4951 (σύρω) through its congener (to fasten;</source>
G4577.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to G138</def> (αἱρέομαι)); a chain (as binding or drawing)</meaning>

G4578.meaning: <meaning><def>a commotion</def>, i.e. (of the air) a gale, (of the ground) an earthquake</meaning>

G4579.meaning: <meaning><def>to rock</def> (vibrate, properly, sideways or to and fro), i.e. (generally) to agitate (in any direction; cause to tremble); figuratively, to throw into a tremor (of fear or concern)</meaning>

G4582.meaning: <meaning><def>probably akin to the alternate of G138</def> (αἱρέομαι), through the idea of attractiveness); the moon</meaning>

G4583.source: <source>middle voice or passive from a presumed derivative of G4582 (σελήνη);</source>

G4592.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of the base of G4591 (σημαίνω);</source>

G4594.source: <source>neuter (as adverb) of a presumed compound of the article G3588 (ὁ) (t changed to s) and G2250 (ἡμέρα);</source>
G4594.meaning: <meaning><def>on the</def> (i.e. this) day (or night current or just passed); generally, now (i.e. at present, hitherto)</meaning>

G4596.source: <source>from (an Indian tribe from whom silk was procured;</source>
G4596.meaning: <meaning><def>hence the name of the silk-worm)</def>; Seric, i.e. silken (neuter as noun, a silky fabric)</meaning>

G4598.source: <source>from G4597 (σής) and a derivative of G977 (βιβρώσκω);</source>

G4599.meaning: <meaning><def>probably akin to the base of G2476</def> (ἵστημι)); to strengthen, i.e. (figuratively) confirm (in spiritual knowledge and power)</meaning>

G4600.meaning: <meaning><def>the jaw-bone</def>, i.e. (by implication) the cheek or side of the face</meaning>

G4602.source: <source>apparently from (to hiss, i.e. hist or hush);</source>

G4607.meaning: <meaning><def>a dagger-man or assassin</def>; a freebooter (Jewish fanatic outlawed by the Romans)</meaning>

G4612.meaning: <meaning><def>a semicinctium or half-girding</def>, i.e. narrow covering (apron)</meaning>

G4613.meaning: <meaning><def>Simon</def> (i.e. Shimon), the name of nine Israelites</meaning>

G4616.meaning: <meaning><def>byssos</def>, i.e. bleached linen (the cloth or a garment of it)</meaning>

G4620.meaning: <meaning><def>a grain-measure</def>, i.e. (by implication) ration (allowance of food)</meaning>

G4622.meaning: <meaning><def>Sion</def> (i.e. Tsijon), a hill of Jerusalem; figuratively, the Church (militant or triumphant)</meaning>

G4623.meaning: <meaning><def>properly</def>, muteness, i.e. involuntary stillness, or inability to speak; and thus differing from G4602 (σιγή), which is rather a voluntary refusal or indisposition to speak, although the terms are often used synonymously); to be dumb (but not deaf also, like 2974 properly); figuratively, to be calm (as quiet water)</meaning>

G4624.meaning: <meaning><def>to entrap</def>, i.e. trip up (figuratively, stumble (transitively) or entice to sin, apostasy or displeasure)</meaning>

G4625.source: <source>("scandal"); probably from a derivative of G2578 (κάμπτω);</source>
G4625.meaning: <meaning><def>a trap-stick</def> (bent sapling), i.e. snare (figuratively, cause of displeasure or sin)</meaning>
G4625.usage: <usage>occasion to fall (of stumbling), offence, thing that offends, stumblingblock.</usage>

G4627.meaning: <meaning><def>a "skiff"</def> (as if dug out), or yawl (carried aboard a large vessel for landing)</meaning>

G4628.meaning: <meaning><def>through the idea of leanness)</def>; the leg (as lank)</meaning>

G4629.meaning: <meaning><def>perhaps akin to the base of G4649</def> (σκοπός) through the idea of noticeableness); clothing</meaning>

G4632.meaning: <meaning><def>a vessel</def>, implement, equipment or apparatus (literally or figuratively [specially, a wife as contributing to the usefulness of the husband])</meaning>

G4633.meaning: <meaning><def>a tent or cloth hut</def> (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G4634.meaning: <meaning><def>the Festival of Tabernacles</def> (so called from the custom of erecting booths for temporary homes)</meaning>

G4636.meaning: <meaning><def>a hut or temporary residence</def>, i.e. (figuratively) the human body (as the abode of the spirit)</meaning>

G4637.meaning: <meaning><def>to tent or encamp</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to occupy (as a mansion) or (specially), to reside (as God did in the Tabernacle of old, a symbol of protection and communion)</meaning>

G4638.meaning: <meaning><def>an encampment</def>, i.e. (figuratively) the Temple (as God's residence), the body (as a tenement for the soul)</meaning>

G4639.meaning: <meaning><def>"shade" or a shadow</def> (literally or figuratively (darkness of error or an adumbration))</meaning>

G4640.meaning: <meaning><def>to jump</def>, i.e. sympathetically move (as the quickening of a fetus)</meaning>

G4641.source: <source>feminine of a compound of G4642 (σκληρός) and G2588 (καρδία);</source>

G4642.meaning: <meaning><def>dry</def>, i.e. hard or tough (figuratively, harsh, severe)</meaning>

G4647.source: <source>perhaps from the base of G4628 (σκέλος) and G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι);</source>
G4647.meaning: <meaning><def>withered at the front</def>, i.e. a point or prickle (figuratively, a bodily annoyance or disability)</meaning>

G4649.meaning: <meaning><def>perhaps akin to G4626</def> (σκάπτω) through the idea of concealment; compare G4629 (σκέπασμα)); a watch (sentry or scout), i.e. (by implication) a goal</meaning>

G4650.source: <source>apparently from the same as G4651 (σκορπίος) (through the idea of penetrating);</source>
G4650.meaning: <meaning><def>to dissipate</def>, i.e. (figuratively) put to flight, waste, be liberal</meaning>

G4651.source: <source>probably from an obsolete (perhaps strengthened from the base of G4649 (σκοπός) and meaning to pierce);</source>

G4657.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of G1519 (εἰς) and G2965 (κύων) and G906 (βάλλω);</source>
G4657.meaning: <meaning><def>what is thrown to the dogs</def>, i.e. refuse (ordure)</meaning>

G4658.meaning: <meaning><def>a Scythene or Scythian</def>, i.e. (by implication) a savage</meaning>

G4659.meaning: <meaning><def>angry-visaged</def>, i.e. gloomy or affecting a mournful appearance</meaning>

G4662.source: <source>from G4663 (σκώληξ) and a derivative of G977 (βιβρώσκω);</source>

G4665.meaning: <meaning><def>the emerald or green gem so called</def></meaning>

G4669.meaning: <meaning><def>to tincture with myrrh</def>, i.e. embitter (as a narcotic)</meaning>

G4673.source: <source>probably akin to the base of G4987 (σωρεύω);</source>
G4673.meaning: <meaning><def>a funereal receptacle</def> (urn, coffin), i.e. (by analogy) a bier</meaning>

G4676.meaning: <meaning><def>a sudarium</def> (sweat-cloth), i.e. towel (for wiping the perspiration from the face, or binding the face of a corpse)</meaning>

G4679.meaning: <meaning><def>to render wise</def>; in a sinister acceptation, to form "sophisms", i.e. continue plausible error</meaning>

G4682.meaning: <meaning><def>apparently strengthened from G4685</def> (σπάω) through the idea of spasmodic contraction); to mangle, i.e. convluse with epilepsy</meaning>

G4683.meaning: <meaning><def>from a derivative of the base of G4682</def> (σπαράσσω) meaning to strap or wrap with strips); to swathe (an infant after the Oriental custom)</meaning>

G4686.source: <source>of immediate Latin origin, but ultimately a derivative of G138 (αἱρέομαι) in the sense of its cognate G1507 (εἱλίσσω);</source>
G4686.meaning: <meaning><def>a coil</def> (spira, "spire"), i.e. (figuratively) a mass of men (a Roman military cohort; also (by analogy) a squad of Levitical janitors)</meaning>

G4687.source: <source>probably strengthened from G4685 (σπάω) (through the idea of extending);</source>

G4688.meaning: <meaning><def>a speculator</def>, i.e. military scout (spy or (by extension) life-guardsman)</meaning>

G4689.meaning: <meaning><def>to pour out as a libation</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to devote (one's life or blood, as a sacrifice) ("spend")</meaning>

G4690.meaning: <meaning><def>something sown</def>, i.e. seed (including the male "sperm"); by implication, offspring; specially, a remnant (figuratively, as if kept over for planting)</meaning>

G4691.meaning: <meaning><def>a seed-picker</def> (as the crow), i.e. (figuratively) a sponger, loafer (specially, a gossip or trifler in talk)</meaning>

G4692.meaning: <meaning><def>to "speed"</def> ("study"), i.e. urge on (diligently or earnestly); by implication, to await eagerly</meaning>

G4693.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of (a grotto);</source>
G4693.meaning: <meaning><def>a cavern</def>; by implication, a hiding-place or resort</meaning>

G4694.meaning: <meaning><def>a ledge or reef of rock in the sea</def></meaning>

G4696.meaning: <meaning><def>a stain or blemish</def>, i.e. (figuratively) defect, disgrace</meaning>

G4697.meaning: <meaning><def>to have the bowels yearn</def>, i.e. (figuratively) feel sympathy, to pity</meaning>

G4704.meaning: <meaning><def>to use speed</def>, i.e. to make effort, be prompt or earnest</meaning>
G4704.usage: <usage>do (give) diligence, be diligent (forward), endeavour, labour, study.</usage>

G4706.meaning: <meaning><def>more earnestly than others)</def>, i.e. very promptly</meaning>

G4712.meaning: <meaning><def>a stade or certain measure of distance</def>; by implication, a stadium or race-course</meaning>

G4714.meaning: <meaning><def>a standing</def> (properly, the act), i.e. (by analogy) position (existence); by implication, a popular uprising; figuratively, controversy</meaning>

G4715.meaning: <meaning><def>a stander</def> (standard of value), i.e. (specially), a stater or certain coin</meaning>

G4716.meaning: <meaning><def>a stake or post</def> (as set upright), i.e. (specially), a pole or cross (as an instrument of capital punishment); figuratively, exposure to death, i.e. self-denial; by implication, the atonement of Christ</meaning>

G4717.meaning: <meaning><def>to impale on the cross</def>; figuratively, to extinguish (subdue) passion or selfishness</meaning>

G4719.meaning: <meaning><def>a head of grain</def> (as standing out from the stalk)</meaning>

G4721.source: <source>strengthened from a primary (a "thatch" or "deck" of a building);</source>

G4722.meaning: <meaning><def>to roof over</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to cover with silence (endure patiently)</meaning>

G4723.source: <source>a contraction from G4731 (στερεός) (as stiff and unnatural);</source>

G4724.source: <source>probably strengthened from the base of G2476 (ἵστημι);</source>
G4724.meaning: <meaning><def>properly</def>, to set fast ("stall"), i.e. (figuratively) to repress (reflexively, abstain from associating with)</meaning>

G4727.meaning: <meaning><def>to make</def> (intransitively, be) in straits, i.e. (by implication) to sigh, murmur, pray inaudibly</meaning>

G4730.source: <source>from a compound of G4728 (στενός) and G5561 (χώρα);</source>

G4735.source: <source>from an apparently primary (to twine or wreathe);</source>
G4735.meaning: <meaning><def>a chaplet</def> (as a badge of royalty, a prize in the public games or a symbol of honor generally; but more conspicuous and elaborate than the simple fillet, G1238 (διάδημα)), literally or figuratively</meaning>

G4737.meaning: <meaning><def>to adorn with an honorary wreath</def> (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G4741.source: <source>from a presumed derivative of G2476 (ἵστημι) (like G4731 (στερεός));</source>
G4741.meaning: <meaning><def>to set fast</def>, i.e. (literally) to turn resolutely in a certain direction, or (figuratively) to confirm</meaning>

G4742.meaning: <meaning><def>a mark incised or punched</def> (for recognition of ownership), i.e. (figuratively) scar of service</meaning>

G4743.meaning: <meaning><def>a point of time</def>, i.e. an instant</meaning>

G4746.meaning: <meaning><def>a spread</def> (as if tramped flat) of loose materials for a couch, i.e. (by implication) a bough of a tree so employed</meaning>

G4747.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of the base of G4748 (στοιχέω);</source>
G4747.meaning: <meaning><def>something orderly in arrangement</def>, i.e. (by implication) a serial (basal, fundamental, initial) constituent (literally), proposition (figuratively)</meaning>

G4748.source: <source>from a derivative of (to range in regular line);</source>
G4748.meaning: <meaning><def>to march in</def> (military) rank (keep step), i.e. (figuratively) to conform to virtue and piety</meaning>

G4749.meaning: <meaning><def>equipment</def>, i.e. (specially), a "stole" or long-fitting gown (as a mark of dignity)</meaning>

G4750.source: <source>probably strengthened from a presumed derivative of the base of G5114 (τομώτερος);</source>
G4750.meaning: <meaning><def>the mouth</def> (as if a gash in the face); by implication, language (and its relations); figuratively, an opening (in the earth); specially, the front or edge (of a weapon)</meaning>

G4752.meaning: <meaning><def>military service</def>, i.e. (figuratively) the apostolic career (as one of hardship and danger)</meaning>

G4753.meaning: <meaning><def>an armament</def>, i.e. (by implication) a body of troops (more or less extensive or systematic)</meaning>

G4754.source: <source>middle voice from the base of G4756 (στρατιά);</source>
G4754.meaning: <meaning><def>to serve in a military campaign</def>; figuratively, to execute the apostolate (with its arduous duties and functions), to contend with carnal inclinations</meaning>

G4755.source: <source>from the base of G4756 (στρατιά) and G71 (ἄγω) or G2233 (ἡγέομαι);</source>
G4755.meaning: <meaning><def>a general</def>, i.e. (by implication or analogy) a (military) governor (prætor), the chief (præfect) of the (Levitical) temple-wardens</meaning>

G4756.meaning: <meaning><def>from the base of G4766</def> (στρώννυμι), as encamped); camp-likeness, i.e. an army, i.e. (figuratively) the angels, the celestial luminaries</meaning>

G4757.source: <source>from a presumed derivative of the same as G4756 (στρατιά);</source>
G4757.meaning: <meaning><def>a camper-out</def>, i.e. a (common) warrior (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G4758.source: <source>from a compound of the base of G4756 (στρατιά) and G3004 (λέγω) (in its original sense);</source>
G4758.meaning: <meaning><def>to gather</def> (or select) as a warrior, i.e. enlist in the army</meaning>

G4759.meaning: <meaning><def>a ruler of an army</def>, i.e. (specially), a Prætorian præfect</meaning>

G4760.source: <source>from the base of G4756 (στρατιά) and the same as G3977 (πεδινός);</source>
G4760.meaning: <meaning><def>a camping-ground</def>, i.e. (by implication) a body of troops</meaning>

G4761.meaning: <meaning><def>to wrench</def>, i.e. (specially), to torture (by the rack), but only figuratively, to pervert</meaning>

G4762.meaning: <meaning><def>to twist</def>, i.e. turn quite around or reverse (literally or figuratively)</meaning>
G4762.usage: <usage>convert, turn (again, back again, self, self about).</usage>

G4766.source: <source>prolongation from a still simpler , (used only as an alternate in certain tenses) (probably akin to G4731 (στερεός) through the idea of positing);</source>
G4766.meaning: <meaning><def>to "strew," i.e. spread</def> (as a carpet or couch)</meaning>

G4767.source: <source>from a derivative of an obsolete apparently primary (to hate);</source>

G4768.meaning: <meaning><def>to render gloomy</def>, i.e. (by implication) glower (be overcast with clouds, or sombreness of speech)</meaning>

G4769.meaning: <meaning><def>properly akin to the base of G2476</def> (ἵστημι)); a post ("style"), i.e. (figuratively) support</meaning>

G4770.meaning: <meaning><def>a "Stoic"</def> (as occupying a particular porch in Athens), i.e. adherent of a certin philosophy</meaning>

G4771.source: <source>the personal pronoun of the second person singular;</source>

G4773.meaning: <meaning><def>a relative</def> (by blood); by extension, a fellow countryman</meaning>

G4774.source: <source>from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G1097 (γινώσκω);</source>

G4776.meaning: <meaning><def>to give</def> (or take) a seat in company with</meaning>

G4778.meaning: <meaning><def>to maltreat in company with</def>, i.e. (passively) endure persecution together</meaning>

G4783.meaning: <meaning><def>a deposition</def> (of sentiment) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) accord with</meaning>

G4784.meaning: <meaning><def>to deposit</def> (one's vote or opinion) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to accord with</meaning>

G4785.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a compound of G2596 (κατά) and G5585 (ψηφίζω);</source>
G4785.meaning: <meaning><def>to count down in company with</def>, i.e. enroll among</meaning>

G4786.meaning: <meaning><def>to commingle</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to combine or assimilate</meaning>

G4787.meaning: <meaning><def>to move together</def>, i.e. (specially), to excite as a mass (to sedition)</meaning>

G4788.meaning: <meaning><def>to shut together</def>, i.e. include or (figuratively) embrace in a common subjection to</meaning>

G4789.meaning: <meaning><def>a co-heir</def>, i.e. (by analogy) participant in common</meaning>

G4790.meaning: <meaning><def>to share in company with</def>, i.e. co-participate in</meaning>

G4792.meaning: <meaning><def>to convey together</def>, i.e. collect or bear away in company with others</meaning>

G4793.meaning: <meaning><def>to judge of one thing in connection with another</def>, i.e. combine (spiritual ideas with appropriate expressions) or collate (one person with another by way of contrast or resemblance)</meaning>

G4794.meaning: <meaning><def>to stoop altogether</def>, i.e. be completely overcome by</meaning>

G4795.source: <source>from a comparative of G4862 (σύν) and (to light or happen;</source>
G4795.meaning: <meaning><def>from the base of G2962</def> (κύριος)); concurrence, i.e. accident</meaning>

G4797.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and (to pour) or its alternate;</source>
G4797.meaning: <meaning><def>to commingle promiscuously</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to throw (an assembly) into disorder, to perplex (the mind)</meaning>
G4797.usage: <usage>confound, confuse, stir up, be in an uproar.</usage>

G4798.meaning: <meaning><def>to use jointly</def>, i.e. (by implication) to hold intercourse in common</meaning>

G4800.meaning: <meaning><def>to continue to live in common with</def>, i.e. co-survive (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G4801.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G2201 (ζεῦγος);</source>

G4802.meaning: <meaning><def>to investigate jointly</def>, i.e. discuss, controvert, cavil</meaning>

G4805.meaning: <meaning><def>co-yoked</def>, i.e. (figuratively) as noun, a colleague; probably rather as a proper name; Syzygus, a Christian</meaning>

G4807.source: <source>of Hebrew origin (H08256) in imitation of G4809 (συκομωραία);</source>

G4811.source: <source>from a compound of G4810 (σῦκον) and a derivative of G5316 (φαίνω);</source>
G4811.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a fig-informer</def> (reporter of the law forbidding the exportation of figs from Greece), "sycophant", i.e. (genitive and by extension) to defraud (exact unlawfully, extort)</meaning>

G4812.source: <source>from the base of G4813 (συλάω) and (the reduplicated form of) G71 (ἄγω);</source>
G4812.meaning: <meaning><def>to lead away as booty</def>, i.e. (figuratively) seduce</meaning>

G4813.meaning: <meaning><def>probably akin to G138</def> (αἱρέομαι); compare G4661 (σκῦλον)); to despoil</meaning>

G4815.meaning: <meaning><def>to clasp</def>, i.e. seize (arrest, capture); specially, to conceive (literally or figuratively); by implication, to aid</meaning>

G4816.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and G3004 (λέγω) in its original sense;</source>

G4818.meaning: <meaning><def>to afflict jointly</def>, i.e. (passive) sorrow at (on account of) someone</meaning>

G4819.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G939 (βάσις);</source>
G4819.meaning: <meaning><def>to walk</def> (figuratively, transpire) together, i.e. concur (take place)</meaning>

G4820.meaning: <meaning><def>to combine</def>, i.e. (in speaking) to converse, consult, dispute, (mentally) to consider, (by implication) to aid, (personally) to join, attack</meaning>
G4820.usage: <usage>confer, encounter, help, make, meet with, ponder.</usage>

G4822.meaning: <meaning><def>causative</def> (by reduplication) of the base of G939 (βάσις)); to drive together, i.e. unite (in association or affection), (mentally) to infer, show, teach</meaning>
G4822.usage: <usage>compact, assuredly gather, intrust, knit together, prove.</usage>

G4823.meaning: <meaning><def>to give</def> (or take) advice jointly, i.e. recommend, deliberate or determine</meaning>

G4824.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of G4825 (σύμβουλος);</source>
G4824.meaning: <meaning><def>advisement</def>; specially, a deliberative body, i.e. the provincial assessors or lay-court</meaning>

G4826.meaning: <meaning><def>Symeon</def> (i.e. Shimon), the name of five Israelites</meaning>

G4827.source: <source>from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3129 (μανθάνω);</source>

G4828.meaning: <meaning><def>to testify jointly</def>, i.e. corroborate by (concurrent) evidence</meaning>

G4831.source: <source>from a presumed compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3401 (μιμέομαι);</source>

G4834.meaning: <meaning><def>to feel "sympathy" with</def>, i.e. (by implication) to commiserate</meaning>
G4834.usage: <usage>have compassion, be touched with a feeling of.</usage>

G4835.meaning: <meaning><def>having a fellow-feeling</def> ("sympathetic"), i.e. (by implication) mutually commiserative</meaning>

G4836.meaning: <meaning><def>to be present together</def>, i.e. to convene; by implication, to appear in aid</meaning>

G4839.meaning: <meaning><def>to remain in company</def>, i.e. still live</meaning>

G4840.meaning: <meaning><def>to be at hand together</def>, i.e. now present</meaning>

G4841.meaning: <meaning><def>to experience pain jointly or of the same kind</def> (specially, persecution; to "sympathize")</meaning>

G4843.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a compound of G4012 (περί) and G2983 (λαμβάνω);</source>
G4843.meaning: <meaning><def>to take by enclosing altogether</def>, i.e. earnestly throw the arms about one</meaning>

G4845.meaning: <meaning><def>to implenish completely</def>, i.e. (of space) to swamp (a boat), or (of time) to accomplish (passive, be complete)</meaning>

G4846.meaning: <meaning><def>to strangle completely</def>, i.e. (literally) to drown, or (figuratively) to crowd</meaning>

G4847.meaning: <meaning><def>a native of the same town</def>, i.e. (figuratively) co-religionist (fellow-Christian)</meaning>

G4848.meaning: <meaning><def>to journey together</def>; by implication, to assemble</meaning>

G4849.source: <source>neuter of a derivative of the alternate of G4844 (συμπίνω);</source>
G4849.meaning: <meaning><def>a drinking-party</def> ("symposium"), i.e. (by extension) a room of guests</meaning>

G4851.meaning: <meaning><def>to bear together</def> (contribute), i.e. (literally) to collect, or (figuratively) to conduce; especially (neuter participle as a noun) advantage</meaning>
G4851.usage: <usage>be better for, bring together, be expedient (for), be good, (be) profit(-able for).</usage>

G4853.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G5443 (φυλή);</source>
G4853.meaning: <meaning><def>a co-tribesman</def>, i.e. native of the same country</meaning>

G4854.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G5453 (φύω);</source>
G4854.meaning: <meaning><def>grown along with</def> (connate), i.e. (figuratively) closely united to</meaning>

G4856.meaning: <meaning><def>to be harmonious</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to accord (be suitable, concur) or stipulate (by compact)</meaning>

G4858.meaning: <meaning><def>unison of sound</def> ("symphony"), i.e. a concert of instruments (harmonious note)</meaning>

G4859.meaning: <meaning><def>sounding together</def> (alike), i.e. (figuratively) accordant (neuter as noun, agreement)</meaning>

G4862.meaning: <meaning><def>with or together</def> (but much closer than G3326 (μετά) or G3844 (παρά)), i.e. by association, companionship, process, resemblance, possession, instrumentality, addition, etc.</meaning>

G4863.meaning: <meaning><def>to lead together</def>, i.e. collect or convene; specially, to entertain (hospitably)</meaning>
G4863.usage: <usage>+ accompany, assemble (selves, together), bestow, come together, gather (selves together, up, together), lead into, resort, take in.</usage>

G4864.meaning: <meaning><def>an assemblage of persons</def>; specially, a Jewish "synagogue" (the meeting or the place); by analogy, a Christian church</meaning>

G4865.meaning: <meaning><def>to struggle in company with</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to be a partner (assistant)</meaning>

G4866.meaning: <meaning><def>to wrestle in company with</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to seek jointly</meaning>

G4868.meaning: <meaning><def>to make up together</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to compute (an account)</meaning>

G4873.meaning: <meaning><def>to recline in company with</def> (at a meal)</meaning>
G4873.usage: <usage>sit (down, at the table, together) with (at meat).</usage>

G4874.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a compound of G303 (ἀνά) and G3396 (μίγνυμι);</source>
G4874.meaning: <meaning><def>to mix up together</def>, i.e. (figurative) associate with</meaning>

G4876.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G473 (ἀντί);</source>

G4878.meaning: <meaning><def>to take hold of opposite together</def>, i.e. co-operate (assist)</meaning>

G4879.meaning: <meaning><def>to take off together</def>, i.e. transport with (seduce, passively, yield)</meaning>

G4880.meaning: <meaning><def>to decease</def> (literally) in company with, or (figuratively), similarly to</meaning>

G4881.meaning: <meaning><def>to destroy</def> (middle voice or passively, be slain) in company with</meaning>

G4882.meaning: <meaning><def>to despatch</def> (on an errand) in company with</meaning>

G4883.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a compound of G719 (ἁρμός) and G3004 (λέγω) (in its original sense of laying);</source>
G4883.meaning: <meaning><def>to render close-jointed together</def>, i.e. organize compactly</meaning>

G4886.meaning: <meaning><def>a joint tie</def>, i.e. ligament, (figuratively) uniting principle, control</meaning>

G4887.meaning: <meaning><def>to bind with</def>, i.e. (passively) be a fellow-prisoner (figuratively)</meaning>

G4888.meaning: <meaning><def>to exalt to dignity in company</def> (i.e. similarly) with</meaning>

G4889.meaning: <meaning><def>a co-slave</def>, i.e. servitor or ministrant of the same master (human or divine)</meaning>

G4891.meaning: <meaning><def>to rouse</def> (from death) in company with, i.e. (figuratively) to revivify (spirtually) in resemblance to</meaning>

G4892.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of a compound of G4862 (σύν) and the base of G1476 (ἑδραῖος);</source>
G4892.meaning: <meaning><def>a joint session</def>, i.e. (specially), the Jewish Sanhedrin; by analogy, a subordinate tribunal</meaning>

G4894.meaning: <meaning><def>to see completely</def>; used (like its primary) only in two past tenses, respectively meaning to understand or become aware, and to be conscious or (clandestinely) informed of</meaning>
G4894.usage: <usage>consider, know, be privy, be ware of.</usage>

G4895.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and G1510 (εἰμί) (including its various inflections);</source>
G4895.meaning: <meaning><def>to be in company with</def>, i.e. present at the time</meaning>

G4898.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G1553 (ἐκδημέω);</source>

G4899.source: <source>from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G1586 (ἐκλέγομαι);</source>
G4899.meaning: <meaning><def>chosen in company with</def>, i.e. co-elect (fellow Christian)</meaning>

G4901.meaning: <meaning><def>to testify further jointly</def>, i.e. unite in adding evidence</meaning>

G4902.source: <source>middle voice from G4862 (σύν) and a primary (to follow);</source>

G4904.source: <source>from a presumed compound of G4862 (σύν) and the base of G2041 (ἔργον);</source>
G4904.usage: <usage>companion in labour, (fellow-)helper(-labourer, -worker), labourer together with, workfellow.</usage>

G4905.meaning: <meaning><def>to convene</def>, depart in company with, associate with, or (specially), cohabit (conjugally)</meaning>
G4905.usage: <usage>accompany, assemble (with), come (together), come (company, go) with, resort.</usage>

G4907.meaning: <meaning><def>a mental putting together</def>, i.e. intelligence or (concretely) the intellect</meaning>

G4909.meaning: <meaning><def>to think well of in common</def>, i.e. assent to, feel gratified with</meaning>

G4910.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a presumed compound of G2095 (εὖ) and a derivative of G2192 (ἔχω) (meaning to be in good condition, i.e. (by implication) to fare well, or feast);</source>
G4910.meaning: <meaning><def>to entertain sumptuously in company with</def>, i.e. (middle voice or passive) to revel together</meaning>

G4911.meaning: <meaning><def>to stand up together</def>, i.e. to resist (or assault) jointly</meaning>

G4912.meaning: <meaning><def>to hold together</def>, i.e. to compress (the ears, with a crowd or siege) or arrest (a prisoner); figuratively, to compel, perplex, afflict, preoccupy</meaning>
G4912.usage: <usage>constrain, hold, keep in, press, lie sick of, stop, be in a strait, straiten, be taken with, throng.</usage>

G4913.source: <source>middle voice from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G2237 (ἡδονή);</source>
G4913.meaning: <meaning><def>to rejoice in with oneself</def>, i.e. feel satisfaction concerning</meaning>

G4914.source: <source>from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G2239 (ἦθος);</source>

G4915.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G2244 (ἡλικία);</source>
G4915.meaning: <meaning><def>a co-aged person</def>, i.e. alike in years</meaning>

G4916.meaning: <meaning><def>to inter in company with</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to assimilate spiritually (to Christ by a sepulture as to sin)</meaning>

G4918.meaning: <meaning><def>to compress</def>, i.e. crowd on all sides</meaning>

G4920.meaning: <meaning><def>to put together</def>, i.e. (mentally) to comprehend; by implication, to act piously</meaning>

G4921.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and G2476 (ἵστημι) (including its collateral forms);</source>
G4921.meaning: <meaning><def>to set together</def>, i.e. (by implication) to introduce (favorably), or (figuratively) to exhibit; intransitively, to stand near, or (figuratively) to constitute</meaning>

G4923.source: <source>from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3598 (ὁδός) ("synod");</source>
G4923.meaning: <meaning><def>companionship on a journey</def>, i.e. (by implication), a caravan</meaning>

G4925.meaning: <meaning><def>to construct</def>, i.e. (passively) to compose (in company with other Christians, figuratively)</meaning>

G4927.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a compound of the base of 3674 and the base of G3725 (ὅριον);</source>

G4931.meaning: <meaning><def>to complete entirely</def>; generally, to execute (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G4932.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G5114 (τομώτερος);</source>
G4932.meaning: <meaning><def>to contract by cutting</def>, i.e. (figuratively) do concisely (speedily)</meaning>

G4933.meaning: <meaning><def>to keep closely together</def>, i.e. (by implication) to conserve (from ruin); mentally, to remember (and obey)</meaning>

G4934.meaning: <meaning><def>to place jointly</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to consent (bargain, stipulate), concur</meaning>

G4937.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and the base of G5147 (τρίβος);</source>
G4937.meaning: <meaning><def>to crush completely</def>, i.e. to shatter (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G4938.meaning: <meaning><def>concussion or utter fracture</def> (properly, concretely), i.e. complete ruin</meaning>

G4939.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and G5162 (τροφός) (in a passive sense);</source>

G4943.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and the base of G2041 (ἔργον);</source>

G4944.meaning: <meaning><def>to have</def> (parturition) pangs in company (concert, simultaneously) with, i.e. (figuratively) to sympathize (in expectation of relief from suffering)</meaning>

G4945.source: <source>from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and G3660 (ὀμνύω);</source>
G4945.meaning: <meaning><def>a swearing together</def>, i.e. (by implication) a plot</meaning>

G4947.meaning: <meaning><def>Syria</def> (i.e. Tsyria or Tyre), a region of Asia</meaning>

G4948.meaning: <meaning><def>a Syran</def> (i.e. probably Tyrian), a native of Syria</meaning>

G4949.source: <source>feminine of a compound of G4948 (Σύρος) and the same as G5403 (Φοινίκη);</source>
G4949.meaning: <meaning><def>a Syro-phœnician woman</def>, i.e. a female native of Phœnicia in Syria</meaning>

G4950.meaning: <meaning><def>a shoal</def> (from the sand drawn thither by the waves), i.e. the Syrtis Major or great bay on the north coast of Africa</meaning>

G4952.meaning: <meaning><def>to rend completely</def>, i.e. (by analogy) to convulse violently</meaning>

G4953.source: <source>neuter of a compound of G4862 (σύν) and the base of G4591 (σημαίνω);</source>
G4953.meaning: <meaning><def>a sign in common</def>, i.e. preconcerted signal</meaning>

G4954.meaning: <meaning><def>of a joint body</def>, i.e. (figuratively) a fellow-member of the Christian community</meaning>

G4955.source: <source>from a compound of G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G4714 (στάσις);</source>

G4957.meaning: <meaning><def>to impale in company with</def> (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G4958.meaning: <meaning><def>to send</def> (draw) together, i.e. enwrap (enshroud a corpse for burial), contract (an interval)</meaning>

G4959.meaning: <meaning><def>to moan jointly</def>, i.e. (figuratively) experience a common calamity</meaning>

G4960.meaning: <meaning><def>to file together</def> (as soldiers in ranks), i.e. (figuratively) to correspond to</meaning>

G4961.meaning: <meaning><def>a co-campaigner</def>, i.e. (figuratively) an associate in Christian toil</meaning>

G4962.meaning: <meaning><def>to twist together</def>, i.e. collect (a bundle, a crowd)</meaning>

G4963.meaning: <meaning><def>a twisting together</def>, i.e. (figuratively) a secret coalition, riotous crowd</meaning>

G4964.source: <source>from G4862 (σύν) and a derivative of G4976 (σχῆμα);</source>
G4964.meaning: <meaning><def>to fashion alike</def>, i.e. conform to the same pattern (figuratively)</meaning>

G4966.meaning: <meaning><def>Sychem</def> (i.e. Shekem), the name of a Canaanite and of a place in Palestine</meaning>

G4967.meaning: <meaning><def>butchery</def> (of animals for food or sacrifice, or (figuratively) of men (destruction))</meaning>

G4969.meaning: <meaning><def>to butcher</def> (especially an animal for food or in sacrifice) or (generally) to slaughter, or (specially), to maim (violently)</meaning>

G4970.meaning: <meaning><def>of uncertain derivation) as adverb</def>; vehemently, i.e. in a high degree, much</meaning>

G4972.meaning: <meaning><def>to stamp</def> (with a signet or private mark) for security or preservation (literally or figuratively); by implication, to keep secret, to attest</meaning>
G4972.usage: <usage>(set a, set to) seal up, stop.</usage>

G4973.meaning: <meaning><def>a signet</def> (as fencing in or protecting from misappropriation); by implication, the stamp impressed (as a mark of privacy, or genuineness), literally or figuratively</meaning>

G4974.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative probably of the same as (a ball, "sphere";</source>
G4974.meaning: <meaning><def>compare the feminine </def>, a hammer); the ankle (as globular)</meaning>

G4975.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of the alternate of G2192 (ἔχω) as adverb;</source>

G4976.meaning: <meaning><def>a figure</def> (as a mode or circumstance), i.e. (by implication) external condition</meaning>

G4977.usage: <usage>break, divide, open, rend, make a rent.</usage>

G4978.meaning: <meaning><def>a split or gap</def> ("schism"), literally or figuratively</meaning>

G4979.meaning: <meaning><def>of uncertain derivation)</def>; a rushlet, i.e. grass-withe or tie (generally)</meaning>

G4980.meaning: <meaning><def>to take a holiday</def>, i.e. be at leisure for (by implication, devote oneself wholly to); figuratively, to be vacant (of a house)</meaning>

G4981.source: <source>probably feminine of a presumed derivative of the alternate of G2192 (ἔχω);</source>
G4981.meaning: <meaning><def>properly</def>, loitering (as a withholding of oneself from work) or leisure, i.e. (by implication) a "school" (as vacation from physical employment)</meaning>

G4982.meaning: <meaning><def>to save</def>, i.e. deliver or protect (literally or figuratively)</meaning>
G4982.usage: <usage>heal, preserve, save (self), do well, be (make) whole.</usage>

G4983.meaning: <meaning><def>the body</def> (as a sound whole), used in a very wide application, literally or figuratively</meaning>

G4986.source: <source>from the base of G4982 (σώζω) and G3962 (πατήρ);</source>
G4986.meaning: <meaning><def>of a safe father</def>; Sopatrus, a Christian</meaning>

G4988.source: <source>from the base of G4982 (σώζω) and that of G4599 (σθενόω);</source>

G4991.source: <source>feminine of a derivative of G4990 (σωτήρ) as (properly, abstract) noun;</source>

G4992.source: <source>neuter of the same as G4991 (σωτηρία) as (properly, concretely) noun;</source>

G4993.meaning: <meaning><def>to be of sound mind</def>, i.e. sane, (figuratively) moderate</meaning>
G4993.usage: <usage>be in right mind, be sober (minded), soberly.</usage>

G4994.meaning: <meaning><def>to make of sound mind</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to discipline or correct</meaning>

G4997.meaning: <meaning><def>soundness of mind</def>, i.e. (literally) sanity or (figuratively) self-control</meaning>

G4998.source: <source>from the base of G4982 (σώζω) and that of G5424 (φρήν);</source>
G4998.meaning: <meaning><def>safe</def> (sound) in mind, i.e. self-controlled (moderate as to opinion or passion)</meaning>

G5000.meaning: <meaning><def>the gazelle</def>; Tabitha (i.e. Tabjetha), a Christian female</meaning>

G5001.meaning: <meaning><def>something orderly in arrangement</def> (a troop), i.e. (figuratively) a series or succession</meaning>

G5003.meaning: <meaning><def>to be wretched</def>, i.e. realize one's own misery</meaning>

G5005.source: <source>from the base of G5007 (τάλαντον) and a derivative of the base of G3984 (πεῖρα);</source>

G5007.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of the original form of (to bear;</source>
G5007.meaning: <meaning><def>equivalent to G5342</def> (φέρω)); a balance (as supporting weights), i.e. (by implication) a certain weight (and thence a coin or rather sum of money) or "talent"</meaning>

G5008.meaning: <meaning><def>the fresh</def>, i.e. young girl; talitha (O maiden)</meaning>

G5009.source: <source>neuter contraction of a presumed derivative of (a dispenser or distributor;</source>
G5009.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to </def>, to cut); a dispensary or magazine, i.e. a chamber on the ground-floor or interior of an Oriental house (generally used for storage or privacy, a spot for retirement)</meaning>

G5010.meaning: <meaning><def>regular arrangement</def>, i.e. (in time) fixed succession (of rank or character), official dignity</meaning>

G5011.usage: <usage>base, cast down, humble, of low degree (estate), lowly.</usage>

G5012.source: <source>from a compound of G5011 (ταπεινός) and the base of G5424 (φρήν);</source>
G5012.usage: <usage>humbleness of mind, humility (of mind, loneliness (of mind).</usage>

G5013.meaning: <meaning><def>to depress</def>; figuratively, to humiliate (in condition or heart)</meaning>

G5014.usage: <usage>humiliation, be made low, low estate, vile.</usage>

G5016.meaning: <meaning><def>disturbance</def>, i.e. (of water) roiling, or (of a mob) sedition</meaning>

G5021.source: <source>a prolonged form of a primary verb (which latter appears only in certain tenses);</source>
G5021.meaning: <meaning><def>to arrange in an orderly manner</def>, i.e. assign or dispose (to a certain position or lot)</meaning>

G5023.source: <source>nominative or accusative case neuter plural of G3778 (οὗτος);</source>
G5023.usage: <usage>+ afterward, follow, + hereafter, X him, the same, so, such, that, then, these, they, this, those, thus.</usage>

G5024.source: <source>neuter plural of G3588 (ὁ) and G846 (αὐτός) as adverb;</source>

G5025.source: <source>dative case and accusative case feminine plural respectively of G3778 (οὗτος);</source>
G5025.meaning: <meaning><def>(to or with or by</def>, etc.) these</meaning>

G5026.source: <source>dative case, accusative case and genitive case respectively of the feminine singular of G3778 (οὗτος);</source>
G5026.usage: <usage>her, + hereof, it, that, + thereby, the (same), this (same).</usage>

G5030.meaning: <meaning><def>briefly</def>, i.e. (in time) speedily, or (in manner) rapidly</meaning>

G5032.source: <source>neuter singular of the comparative of G5036 (ταχύς) (as adverb);</source>
G5032.meaning: <meaning><def>more swiftly</def>, i.e. (in manner) more rapidly, or (in time) more speedily</meaning>

G5033.source: <source>neuter plural of the superlative of G5036 (ταχύς) (as adverb);</source>
G5033.meaning: <meaning><def>most quickly</def>, i.e. (with G5613 (ὡς) prefixed) as soon as possible</meaning>

G5034.meaning: <meaning><def>a brief space</def> (of time), i.e. (with G1722 (ἐν) prefixed) in haste</meaning>

G5035.meaning: <meaning><def>shortly</def>, i.e. without delay, soon, or (by surprise) suddenly, or (by implication, of ease) readily</meaning>

G5037.source: <source>a primary particle (enclitic) of connection or addition;</source>
G5037.meaning: <meaning><def>both or also</def> (properly, as correlation of G2532 (καί))</meaning>

G5039.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of (a goal or fixed limit);</source>
G5039.meaning: <meaning><def>a token</def> (as defining a fact), i.e. criterion of certainty</meaning>

G5041.source: <source>from a compound of G5043 (τέκνον) and the base of G1096 (γίνομαι);</source>

G5042.meaning: <meaning><def>childbirth</def> (parentage), i.e. (by implication) maternity (the performance of maternal duties)</meaning>

G5044.source: <source>from a compound of G5043 (τέκνον) and G5142 (τρέφω);</source>
G5044.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a childrearer</def>, i.e. fulfil the duties of a female parent</meaning>

G5045.meaning: <meaning><def>an artificer</def> (as producer of fabrics), i.e. (specially), a craftsman in wood</meaning>

G5046.meaning: <meaning><def>complete</def> (in various applications of labor, growth, mental and moral character, etc.); neuter (as noun, with G3588 (ὁ)) completeness</meaning>

G5048.meaning: <meaning><def>to complete</def>, i.e. (literally) accomplish, or (figuratively) consummate (in character)</meaning>

G5050.meaning: <meaning><def>(the act) completion</def>, i.e. (of prophecy) verification, or (of expiation) absolution</meaning>

G5052.source: <source>from a compound of G5056 (τέλος) and G5342 (φέρω);</source>
G5052.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a bearer to completion</def> (maturity), i.e. to ripen fruit (figuratively)</meaning>

G5053.meaning: <meaning><def>to finish life</def> (by implication, of G979 (βίος)), i.e. expire (demise)</meaning>

G5055.meaning: <meaning><def>to end</def>, i.e. complete, execute, conclude, discharge (a debt)</meaning>
G5055.usage: <usage>accomplish, make an end, expire, fill up, finish, go over, pay, perform.</usage>

G5056.source: <source>from a primary (to set out for a definite point or goal);</source>
G5056.meaning: <meaning><def>properly</def>, the point aimed at as a limit, i.e. (by implication) the conclusion of an act or state (termination (literally, figuratively or indefinitely), result (immediate, ultimate or prophetic), purpose); specially, an impost or levy (as paid)</meaning>

G5057.meaning: <meaning><def>a tax-farmer</def>, i.e. collector of public revenue</meaning>

G5058.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of G5057 (τελώνης);</source>

G5069.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of (a tetrad;</source>
G5069.meaning: <meaning><def>from G5064</def> (τέσσαρες)); a quaternion or squad (picket) of four Roman soldiers</meaning>

G5070.source: <source>from the multiplicative adverb of G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G5507 (χίλιοι);</source>

G5072.source: <source>neuter of a compound of G5064 (τέσσαρες) and G3376 (μήν);</source>

G5073.source: <source>from G5064 (τέσσαρες) and a derivative of the base of G4118 (πλεῖστος);</source>

G5076.meaning: <meaning><def>the ruler of a fourth part of a country</def> ("tetrarch")</meaning>

G5078.meaning: <meaning><def>art</def> (as productive), i.e. (specially), a trade, or (generally) skill</meaning>

G5081.source: <source>adverb from a compound of a derivative of G5056 (τέλος) and G827 (αὐγή);</source>

G5082.source: <source>from a compound of G3588 (ὁ) with G2245 (ἡλίκος) and G3778 (οὗτος);</source>
G5082.meaning: <meaning><def>such as this</def>, i.e. (in (figurative) magnitude) so vast</meaning>

G5083.meaning: <meaning><def>perhaps akin to G2334</def> (θεωρέω)); to guard (from loss or injury, properly, by keeping the eye upon; and thus differing from G5442 (φυλάσσω), which is properly to prevent escaping; and from G2892 (κουστωδία), which implies a fortress or full military lines of apparatus), i.e. to note (a prophecy; figuratively, to fulfil a command); by implication, to detain (in custody; figuratively, to maintain); by extension, to withhold (for personal ends; figuratively, to keep unmarried); by extension, to withhold (for personal ends; figuratively, to keep unmarried)</meaning>

G5084.meaning: <meaning><def>a watching</def>, i.e. (figuratively) observance, or (concretely) a prison</meaning>

G5085.meaning: <meaning><def>Tiberias</def>, the name of a town and a lake in Palestine</meaning>

G5086.meaning: <meaning><def>probably pertaining to the river Tiberis or Tiber</def>; Tiberius, a Roman emperor</meaning>

G5087.source: <source>a prolonged form of a primary (which is used only as alternate in certain tenses);</source>
G5087.meaning: <meaning><def>to place</def> (in the widest application, literally and figuratively; properly, in a passive or horizontal posture, and thus different from G2476 (ἵστημι), which properly denotes an upright and active position, while G2749 (κεῖμαι) is properly reflexive and utterly prostrate)</meaning>
G5087.usage: <usage>+ advise, appoint, bow, commit, conceive, give, X kneel down, lay (aside, down, up), make, ordain, purpose, put, set (forth), settle, sink down.</usage>

G5088.source: <source>a strengthened form of a primary (which is used only as alternate in certain tenses);</source>
G5088.meaning: <meaning><def>to produce</def> (from seed, as a mother, a plant, the earth, etc.), literally or figuratively</meaning>
G5088.usage: <usage>bear, be born, bring forth, be delivered, be in travail.</usage>

G5089.source: <source>perhaps akin to the alternate of G138 (αἱρέομαι), and thus to G4951 (σύρω);</source>

G5091.meaning: <meaning><def>to prize</def>, i.e. fix a valuation upon; by implication, to revere</meaning>

G5092.meaning: <meaning><def>a value</def>, i.e. money paid, or (concretely and collectively) valuables; by analogy, esteem (especially of the highest degree), or the dignity itself</meaning>

G5093.meaning: <meaning><def>valuable</def>, i.e. (objectively) costly, or (subjectively) honored, esteemed, or (figuratively) beloved</meaning>
G5093.usage: <usage>dear, honourable, (more, most) precious, had in reputation.</usage>

G5097.source: <source>from a comparative of G5092 (τιμή) and ouros (a guard);</source>
G5097.meaning: <meaning><def>properly</def>, to protect one's honor, i.e. to avenge (inflict a penalty)</meaning>

G5099.source: <source>strengthened for a primary (which is only used as an alternate in certain tenses);</source>
G5099.meaning: <meaning><def>to pay a price</def>, i.e. as a penalty</meaning>

G5100.usage: <usage>a (kind of), any (man, thing, thing at all), certain (thing), divers, he (every) man, one (X thing), ought, + partly, some (man, -body, - thing, -what), (+ that no-)thing, what(-soever), X wherewith, whom(-soever), whose(-soever).</usage>

G5101.meaning: <meaning><def>an interrogative pronoun</def>, who, which or what (in direct or indirect questions)</meaning>
G5101.usage: <usage>every man, how (much), + no(-ne, thing), what (manner, thing), where (-by, -fore, -of, -unto, - with, -withal), whether, which, who(-m, -se), why.</usage>

G5104.source: <source>probably for the dative case of G3588 (ὁ);</source>
G5104.meaning: <meaning><def>an enclitic particle of asseveration by way of contrast</def>; in sooth</meaning>
G5104.usage: <usage>(used only with other particles in the comparative, as G2544 (καίτοιγε), G3305 (μέντοι), G5105 (τοιγαροῦν), G5106 (τοίνυν), etc.</usage>

G5107.source: <source>(including the other inflections); from a derivative of 5104 and G1161 (δέ);</source>

G5108.source: <source>(including the other inflections); from G5104 (τοί) and G3778 (οὗτος);</source>
G5108.meaning: <meaning><def>truly this</def>, i.e. of this sort (to denote character or individuality)</meaning>

G5111.meaning: <meaning><def>probably itself from the base of G5056</def> (τέλος) through the idea of extreme conduct); to venture (objectively or in act; while G2292 (θαῤῥέω) is rather subjective or in feeling); by implication, to be courageous</meaning>

G5112.source: <source>neuter of the compound of a derivative of the base of 5111 (as adverb);</source>
G5112.meaning: <meaning><def>more daringly</def>, i.e. with greater confidence than otherwise</meaning>

G5114.source: <source>comparative of a derivative of the primary (to cut;</source>
G5114.meaning: <meaning><def>more comprehensive or decisive than G2875</def> (κόπτω), as if by a single stroke; whereas that implies repeated blows, like hacking); more keen</meaning>

G5116.meaning: <meaning><def>of uncertain origin)</def>; a gem, probably the chrysolite</meaning>

G5117.meaning: <meaning><def>a spot</def> (general in space, but limited by occupancy; whereas G5561 (χώρα) is a large but participle locality), i.e. location (as a position, home, tract, etc.); figuratively, condition, opportunity; specially, a scabbard</meaning>
G5117.usage: <usage>coast, licence, place, X plain, quarter, + rock, room, where.</usage>

G5118.meaning: <meaning><def>apparently from G3588</def> (ὁ) and G3739 (ὅς)) and G3778 (οὗτος) (including its variations); so vast as this, i.e. such (in quantity, amount, number of space)</meaning>
G5118.usage: <usage>as large, so great (long, many, much), these many.</usage>

G5119.meaning: <meaning><def>the when</def>, i.e. at the time that (of the past or future, also in consecution)</meaning>

G5120.meaning: <meaning><def>sometimes used for G5127</def> (τούτου); of this person</meaning>

G5121.source: <source>contraction for the neuter of G3588 (ὁ) and G1726 (ἐναντίον);</source>

G5122.source: <source>contraction for the neuter of G3588 (ὁ) and G3686 (ὄνομα);</source>

G5124.source: <source>neuter singular nominative or accusative case of G3778 (οὗτος);</source>
G5124.usage: <usage>here (-unto), it, partly, self(-same), so, that (intent), the same, there(-fore, -unto), this, thus, where(-fore).</usage>

G5125.source: <source>dative case plural masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος);</source>
G5125.meaning: <meaning><def>to</def> (for, in, with or by) these (persons or things)</meaning>

G5126.meaning: <meaning><def>this</def> (person, as objective of verb or preposition)</meaning>

G5127.source: <source>genitive case singular masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος);</source>
G5127.usage: <usage>here(-by), him, it, + such manner of, that, thence(-forth), thereabout, this, thus.</usage>

G5128.meaning: <meaning><def>these</def> (persons, as objective of verb or preposition)</meaning>

G5129.source: <source>dative case singular masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος);</source>
G5129.meaning: <meaning><def>to</def> (in, with or by) this (person or thing)</meaning>
G5129.usage: <usage>here(-by, -in), him, one, the same, there(-in), this.</usage>

G5130.source: <source>genitive case plural masculine or neuter of G3778 (οὗτος);</source>
G5130.usage: <usage>such, their, these (things), they, this sort, those.</usage>

G5132.meaning: <meaning><def>a table or stool</def> (as being four-legged), usually for food (figuratively, a meal); also a counter for money (figuratively, a broker's office for loans at interest)</meaning>

G5134.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to the base of G2352</def> (θραύω), G5147 (τρίβος), G5149 (τρίζω), etc.); a wound</meaning>

G5136.meaning: <meaning><def>to seize by the throat or neck</def>, i.e. to expose the gullet of a victim for killing (generally, to lay bare)</meaning>

G5137.source: <source>probably from G5143 (τρέχω) (through the idea of mobility);</source>

G5138.source: <source>perhaps strengthened from the base of G4486 (ῥήγνυμι) (as if jagged by rents);</source>

G5139.meaning: <meaning><def>rough district</def>; Trachonitis, a region of Syria</meaning>

G5142.meaning: <meaning><def>but perhaps strengthened from the base of G5157</def> (τροπή) through the idea of convolution); properly, to stiffen, i.e. fatten (by implication, to cherish (with food, etc.), pamper, rear)</meaning>

G5143.meaning: <meaning><def>compare G2359</def> (θρίξ)); which uses (the base of G1408 (δρόμος)) as alternate in certain tenses; to run or walk hastily (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G5146.meaning: <meaning><def>properly</def>, a crow-foot (three-pronged obstruction in war), i.e. (by analogy) a thorny plant (caltrop)</meaning>

G5147.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to </def>, , and the base of G5131 (τράγος), G5134 (τραῦμα)); a rut or worn track</meaning>

G5148.source: <source>from a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G2094 (ἔτος);</source>

G5149.meaning: <meaning><def>to creak</def> (squeak), i.e. (by analogy) to grate the teeth (in frenzy)</meaning>

G5150.source: <source>neuter of a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G3376 (μήν) as noun;</source>

G5152.source: <source>neuter of a compound of G5140 (τρεῖς) and G4721 (στέγη) as noun;</source>

G5154.meaning: <meaning><def>third</def>; neuter (as noun) a third part, or (as adverb) a (or the) third time, thirdly</meaning>

G5158.meaning: <meaning><def>a turn</def>, i.e. (by implication) mode or style (especially with preposition or relative prefix as adverb, like); figuratively, deportment or character</meaning>
G5158.usage: <usage>(even) as, conversation, (+ like) manner, (+ by any) means, way.</usage>

G5163.meaning: <meaning><def>a track</def> (as a wheel-rut), i.e. (figuratively) a course of conduct</meaning>

G5164.meaning: <meaning><def>a wheel</def> (as a runner), i.e. (figuratively) a circuit of physical effects</meaning>

G5165.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of uncertain affinity;</source>

G5166.source: <source>from a derivative of (to dry) meaning ripe fruit (as if dry);</source>

G5167.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to G5149</def> (τρίζω), but denoting a duller sound); a turtle-dove (as cooing)</meaning>

G5168.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to the base of G5134</def> (τραῦμα), G5147 (τρίβος) and G5176 (τρώγω)); an orifice, i.e. needle's eye</meaning>

G5169.source: <source>from a derivative of the base of G5168 (τρυμαλιά);</source>

G5172.source: <source>from (to break up or (figuratively) enfeeble, especially the mind and body by indulgence);</source>

G5174.meaning: <meaning><def>the Troad</def> (or plain of Troy), i.e. Troas, a place in Asia Minor</meaning>

G5176.source: <source>probably strengthened from a collateral form of the base of G5134 (τραῦμα) and G5147 (τρίβος) through the idea of corrosion or wear;</source>
G5176.meaning: <meaning><def>or perhaps rather of a base of G5167</def> (τρυγών) and G5149 (τρίζω) through the idea of a craunching sound; to gnaw or chew, i.e. (generally) to eat</meaning>

G5177.source: <source>probably for an obsolete (for which the middle voice of another alternate (to make ready or bring to pass) is used in certain tenses;</source>
G5177.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to the base of G5088</def> (τίκτω) through the idea of effecting; properly, to affect; or (specially), to hit or light upon (as a mark to be reached), i.e. (transitively) to attain or secure an object or end, or (intransitively) to happen (as if meeting with); but in the latter application only impersonal (with G1487 (εἰ)), i.e. perchance; or (present participle) as adjective, usual (as if commonly met with, with G3756 (οὐ), extraordinary), neuter (as adverb) perhaps; or (with another verb) as adverb, by accident (as it were)</meaning>
G5177.usage: <usage>be, chance, enjoy, little, obtain, X refresh…self, + special.</usage>

G5178.source: <source>from a derivative of G5180 (τύπτω) (meaning a drum, "tympanum");</source>
G5178.meaning: <meaning><def>to stretch on an instrument of torture resembling a drum</def>, and thus beat to death</meaning>

G5179.meaning: <meaning><def>a die</def> (as struck), i.e. (by implication) a stamp or scar; by analogy, a shape, i.e. a statue, (figuratively) style or resemblance; specially, a sampler ("type"), i.e. a model (for imitation) or instance (for warning)</meaning>
G5179.usage: <usage>en-(ex-)ample, fashion, figure, form, manner, pattern, print.</usage>

G5180.meaning: <meaning><def>to "thump"</def>, i.e. cudgel or pummel (properly, with a stick or bastinado), but in any case by repeated blows; thus differing from G3817 (παίω) and G3960 (πατάσσω), which denote a (usually single) blow with the hand or any instrument, or G4141 (πλήσσω) with the fist (or a hammer), or G4474 (ῥαπίζω) with the palm; as well as from G5177 (τυγχάνω), an accidental collision); by implication, to punish; figuratively, to offend (the conscience)</meaning>

G5181.source: <source>a provincial form of the derivative of the base of G2962 (κύριος);</source>

G5182.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to G2351</def> (θόρυβος)); to make "turbid", i.e. disturb</meaning>

G5199.meaning: <meaning><def>healthy</def>, i.e. well (in body); figuratively, true (in doctrine)</meaning>

G5202.source: <source>from a compound of G5204 (ὕδωρ) and a derivative of G4095 (πίνω);</source>

G5203.source: <source>from a compound of G5204 (ὕδωρ) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι) (as if looking watery);</source>

G5206.source: <source>from a presumed compound of G5207 (υἱός) and a derivative of G5087 (τίθημι);</source>

G5214.meaning: <meaning><def>to hymn</def>, i.e. sing a religious ode; by implication, to celebrate (God) in song</meaning>

G5215.meaning: <meaning><def>probably akin to G103</def> (ᾄδω); compare H05667); a "hymn" or religious ode (one of the Psalms)</meaning>

G5217.meaning: <meaning><def>to lead</def> (oneself) under, i.e. withdraw or retire (as if sinking out of sight), literally or figuratively</meaning>

G5218.meaning: <meaning><def>attentive hearkening</def>, i.e. (by implication) compliance or submission</meaning>

G5219.meaning: <meaning><def>to hear under</def> (as a subordinate), i.e. to listen attentively; by implication, to heed or conform to a command or authority</meaning>

G5220.meaning: <meaning><def>in subjection under a man</def>, i.e. a married woman</meaning>

G5221.source: <source>from G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of G473 (ἀντί);</source>
G5221.meaning: <meaning><def>to go opposite</def> (meet) under (quietly), i.e. to encounter, fall in with</meaning>

G5222.meaning: <meaning><def>an encounter or concurrence</def> (with G1519 (εἰς) for infinitive, in order to fall in with)</meaning>

G5224.source: <source>neuter plural of present participle active of G5225 (ὑπάρχω) as noun;</source>

G5225.meaning: <meaning><def>to begin under</def> (quietly), i.e. come into existence (be present or at hand); expletively, to exist (as copula or subordinate to an adjective, participle, adverb or preposition, or as an auxiliary to a principal (verb)</meaning>

G5230.source: <source>from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the base of G188 (ἀκμήν);</source>

G5231.meaning: <meaning><def>above upward</def>, i.e. greatly higher (in place or rank)</meaning>

G5233.source: <source>from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the base of G939 (βάσις);</source>

G5234.source: <source>adverb from present participle active of G5235 (ὑπερβάλλω);</source>

G5235.meaning: <meaning><def>to throw beyod the usual mark</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to surpass (only active participle supereminent)</meaning>
