G5238.source: <source>from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the neuter plural of G1565 (ἐκεῖνος);</source>

G5240.source: <source>from G5228 (ὑπέρ) and the alternate form of G1632 (ἐκχέω);</source>
G5240.meaning: <meaning><def>to pour out over</def>, i.e. (passively) to overflow</meaning>

G5242.meaning: <meaning><def>to hold oneself above</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to excel; participle (as adjective, or neuter as noun) superior, superiority</meaning>

G5245.meaning: <meaning><def>to vanquish beyond</def>, i.e. gain a decisive victory</meaning>

G5251.meaning: <meaning><def>to elevate above others</def>, i.e. raise to the highest position</meaning>

G5252.meaning: <meaning><def>to esteem oneself overmuch</def>, i.e. be vain or arrogant</meaning>

G5253.meaning: <meaning><def>a higher part of the house</def>, i.e. apartment in the third story</meaning>

G5254.meaning: <meaning><def>to hold oneself under</def>, i.e. endure with patience</meaning>

G5256.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a subordinate</def>, i.e. (by implication) subserve</meaning>

G5257.source: <source>from G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of (to row);</source>

G5258.source: <source>from an obsolete primary (perhaps akin to G5259 (ὑπό) through the idea of subsilience);</source>

G5259.meaning: <meaning><def>under</def>, i.e. (with the genitive case) of place (beneath), or with verbs (the agency or means, through); (with the accusative case) of place (whither (underneath) or where (below) or time (when (at))</meaning>

G5260.meaning: <meaning><def>to throw in stealthily</def>, i.e. introduce by collusion</meaning>

G5261.source: <source>from a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G1125 (γράφω);</source>

G5262.meaning: <meaning><def>an exhibit for imitation or warning</def> (figuratively, specimen, adumbration)</meaning>

G5263.meaning: <meaning><def>to exhibit under the eyes</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to exemplify (instruct, admonish)</meaning>

G5265.meaning: <meaning><def>to bind under one's feet</def>, i.e. put on shoes or sandals</meaning>

G5268.source: <source>neuter of a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and G2218 (ζυγός);</source>
G5268.meaning: <meaning><def>an animal under the yoke</def> (draught-beast), i.e. (specially), a donkey</meaning>

G5269.meaning: <meaning><def>to gird under</def>, i.e. frap (a vessel with cables across the keel, sides and deck)</meaning>

G5278.meaning: <meaning><def>to stay under</def> (behind), i.e. remain; figuratively, to undergo, i.e. bear (trials), have fortitude, persevere</meaning>

G5287.meaning: <meaning><def>a setting under</def> (support), i.e. (figuratively) concretely, essence, or abstractly, assurance (objectively or subjectively)</meaning>

G5288.meaning: <meaning><def>to withhold under</def> (out of sight), i.e. (reflexively) to cower or shrink, (figuratively) to conceal (reserve)</meaning>

G5290.meaning: <meaning><def>to turn under</def> (behind), i.e. to return (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G5291.meaning: <meaning><def>to strew underneath</def> (the feet as a carpet)</meaning>

G5293.usage: <usage>be under obedience (obedient), put under, subdue unto, (be, make) subject (to, unto), be (put) in subjection (to, under), submit self unto.</usage>

G5294.meaning: <meaning><def>to place underneath</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to hazard, (reflexively) to suggest</meaning>

G5295.meaning: <meaning><def>to run under</def>, i.e. (specially), to sail past</meaning>

G5296.source: <source>from a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of G5179 (τύπος);</source>
G5296.meaning: <meaning><def>typification under</def> (after), i.e. (concretely) a sketch (figuratively) for imitation</meaning>

G5297.meaning: <meaning><def>to bear from underneath</def>, i.e. (figuratively) to undergo hardship</meaning>

G5299.source: <source>from a compound of G5259 (ὑπό) and a derivative of G3700 (ὀπτάνομαι);</source>

G5310.meaning: <meaning><def>highest</def>, i.e. (masculine singular) the Supreme (God), or (neuter plural) the heavens</meaning>

G5318.meaning: <meaning><def>shining</def>, i.e. apparent (literally or figuratively); neuter (as adverb) publicly, externally</meaning>

G5324.meaning: <meaning><def>to make apparent</def>, i.e. (passively) to appear (neuter participle as noun, a spectacle)</meaning>

G5327.source: <source>properly, strengthened from the base of G4008 (πέραν) or rather of G4486 (ῥήγνυμι);</source>

G5334.source: <source>from G5346 (φημί) (not the same as "phase", which is from G5316 (φαίνω));</source>

G5348.meaning: <meaning><def>to be beforehand</def>, i.e. anticipate or precede; by extension, to have arrived at</meaning>

G5351.meaning: <meaning><def>properly</def>, to shrivel or wither, i.e. to spoil (by any process) or (generally) to ruin (especially figuratively, by moral influences, to deprave)</meaning>

G5353.meaning: <meaning><def>utterance</def>, i.e. a musical note (vocal or instrumental)</meaning>

G5355.source: <source>probably akin to the base of G5351 (φθείρω);</source>

G5356.meaning: <meaning><def>decay</def>, i.e. ruin (spontaneous or inflicted, literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G5358.meaning: <meaning><def>fond to good</def>, i.e. a promoter of virtue</meaning>

G5362.meaning: <meaning><def>fond of man</def>, i.e. affectionate as a wife</meaning>

G5364.source: <source>adverb from a compound of G5384 (φίλος) and G444 (ἄνθρωπος);</source>

G5376.meaning: <meaning><def>fond of horses</def>; Philippus, the name of four Israelites</meaning>

G5378.meaning: <meaning><def>fond of words</def>, i.e. talkative (argumentative, learned, "philological"); Philologus, a Christian</meaning>

G5380.meaning: <meaning><def>probably akin to G3534</def> (νῖκος)); fond of strife, i.e. disputatious</meaning>

G5383.source: <source>from a compound of G5384 (φίλος) and G4413 (πρῶτος);</source>
G5383.meaning: <meaning><def>to be fond of being first</def>, i.e. ambitious of distinction</meaning>

G5384.meaning: <meaning><def>actively</def>, fond, i.e. friendly (still as a noun, an associate, neighbor, etc.)</meaning>

G5387.source: <source>from G5384 (φίλος) and (cherishing one's kindred, especially parents or children);</source>

G5389.meaning: <meaning><def>to be fond of honor</def>, i.e. emulous (eager or earnest to do something)</meaning>

G5393.source: <source>active participle of the base of G5395 (φλόξ);</source>

G5394.meaning: <meaning><def>to cause a blaze</def>, i.e. ignite (figuratively, to inflame with passion)</meaning>

G5396.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a babbler or trifler</def>, i.e. (by implication) to berate idly or mischievously</meaning>

G5399.meaning: <meaning><def>to frighten</def>, i.e. (passively) to be alarmed; by analogy, to be in awe of, i.e. revere</meaning>

G5401.source: <source>from a primary (to be put in fear);</source>

G5402.meaning: <meaning><def>probably akin to the base of G5457</def> (φῶς)); Phœbe, a Christian woman</meaning>

G5403.meaning: <meaning><def>palm-country</def>; Phœnice (or Phœnicia), a region of Palestine</meaning>

G5406.meaning: <meaning><def>a murderer</def> (always of criminal (or at least intentional) homicide; which G443 (ἀνθρωποκτόνος) does not necessarily imply; while G4607 (σικάριος) is a special term for a public bandit)</meaning>

G5409.meaning: <meaning><def>to have a burden</def>, i.e. (by analogy) to wear as clothing or a constant accompaniment</meaning>

G5410.meaning: <meaning><def>a forum or market-place</def>; only in comparative with G675 (Ἄππιος); a station on the Appian road</meaning>

G5411.meaning: <meaning><def>a load</def> (as borne), i.e. (figuratively) a tax (properly, an individual assessment on persons or property; whereas G5056 (τέλος) is usually a general toll on goods or travel)</meaning>

G5412.meaning: <meaning><def>to load up</def> (properly, as a vessel or animal), i.e. (figuratively) to overburden with ceremony (or spiritual anxiety)</meaning>

G5413.meaning: <meaning><def>an invoice</def> (as part of freight), i.e. (figuratively) a task or service</meaning>

G5414.meaning: <meaning><def>something carried</def>, i.e. the cargo of a ship</meaning>

G5416.source: <source>neuter of a derivative from the base of G5417 (φραγελλόω);</source>
G5416.meaning: <meaning><def>a whip</def>, i.e. Roman lash as a public punishment</meaning>

G5417.source: <source>from a presumed equivalent of the Latin flagellum;</source>
G5417.meaning: <meaning><def>to whip</def>, i.e. lash as a public punishment</meaning>

G5418.meaning: <meaning><def>a fence</def>, or inclosing barrier (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G5419.source: <source>probably akin to G5420 (φράσσω) through the idea of defining;</source>
G5419.meaning: <meaning><def>to indicate</def> (by word or act), i.e. (specially), to expound</meaning>

G5420.source: <source>apparently a strengthening form of the base of G5424 (φρήν);</source>
G5420.meaning: <meaning><def>to fence or inclose</def>, i.e. (specially), to block up (figuratively, to silence)</meaning>

G5421.meaning: <meaning><def>a hole in the ground</def> (dug for obtaining or holding water or other purposes), i.e. a cistern or well; figuratively, an abyss (as a prison)</meaning>

G5424.source: <source>probably from an obsolete (to rein in or curb; compare G5420 (φράσσω));</source>

G5426.meaning: <meaning><def>to exercise the mind</def>, i.e. entertain or have a sentiment or opinion; by implication, to be (mentally) disposed (more or less earnestly in a certain direction); intensively, to interest oneself in (with concern or obedience)</meaning>

G5428.meaning: <meaning><def>mental action or activity</def>, i.e. intellectual or moral insight</meaning>

G5429.meaning: <meaning><def>thoughtful</def>, i.e. sagacious or discreet (implying a cautious character; while G4680 (σοφός) denotes practical skill or acumen; and G4908 (συνετός) indicates rather intelligence or mental acquirement); in a bad sense conceited (also in the comparative)</meaning>

G5432.source: <source>from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G3708 (ὁράω);</source>
G5432.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a watcher in advance</def>, i.e. to mount guard as a sentinel (post spies at gates); figuratively, to hem in, protect</meaning>

G5433.meaning: <meaning><def>to snort</def> (as a spirited horse), i.e. (figuratively) to make a tumult</meaning>

G5434.source: <source>neuter of a presumed derivative of (to roast or parch;</source>
G5434.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to the base of G5395</def> (φλόξ)); something desiccated, i.e. a dry twig</meaning>

G5438.meaning: <meaning><def>a guarding or</def> (concretely, guard), the act, the person; figuratively, the place, the condition, or (specially), the time (as a division of day or night), literally or figuratively</meaning>

G5440.meaning: <meaning><def>a guard-case</def>, i.e. "phylactery" for wearing slips of Scripture texts</meaning>

G5442.source: <source>probably from G5443 (φυλή) through the idea of isolation;</source>
G5442.meaning: <meaning><def>to watch</def>, i.e. be on guard (literally of figuratively); by implication, to preserve, obey, avoid</meaning>

G5445.source: <source>from a prolonged form of (to mix a liquid with a solid;</source>
G5445.meaning: <meaning><def>perhaps akin to G5453</def> (φύω) through the idea of swelling in bulk), mean to knead; a mass of dough</meaning>

G5448.source: <source>from G5449 (φύσις) in the primary sense of blowing;</source>

G5449.meaning: <meaning><def>growth</def> (by germination or expansion), i.e. (by implication) natural production (lineal descent); by extension, a genus or sort; figuratively, native disposition, constitution or usage</meaning>

G5452.meaning: <meaning><def>to set out in the earth</def>, i.e. implant; figuratively, to instil doctrine</meaning>

G5453.meaning: <meaning><def>probably originally</def>, to "puff" or blow, i.e. to swell up; but only used in the implied sense, to germinate or grow (sprout, produce), literally or figuratively</meaning>

G5455.meaning: <meaning><def>to emit a sound</def> (animal, human or instrumental); by implication, to address in words or by name, also in imitation</meaning>

G5456.source: <source>probably akin to G5316 (φαίνω) through the idea of disclosure;</source>
G5456.meaning: <meaning><def>a tone</def> (articulate, bestial or artificial); by implication, an address (for any purpose), saying or language</meaning>

G5457.source: <source>from an obsolete (to shine or make manifest, especially by rays;</source>
G5457.meaning: <meaning><def>compare G5316</def> (φαίνω), G5346 (φημί)); luminousness (in the widest application, natural or artificial, abstract or concrete, literal or figurative)</meaning>

G5458.meaning: <meaning><def>an illuminator</def>, i.e. (concretely) a luminary, or (abstractly) brilliancy</meaning>

G5461.meaning: <meaning><def>to shed rays</def>, i.e. to shine or (transitively) to brighten up (literally or figuratively)</meaning>
G5461.usage: <usage>enlighten, illuminate, (bring to, give) light, make to see.</usage>

G5463.meaning: <meaning><def>to be "cheer"ful</def>, i.e. calmly happy or well-off; impersonally, especially as salutation (on meeting or parting), be well</meaning>
G5463.usage: <usage>farewell, be glad, God speed, greeting, hall, joy(- fully), rejoice.</usage>

G5466.meaning: <meaning><def>a Chaldæan</def> (i.e. Kasdi), or native or the region of the lower Euphrates</meaning>

G5467.source: <source>perhaps from G5465 (χαλάω) through the idea of reducing the strength;</source>

G5468.source: <source>from a compound of G5469 (χαλινός) and the reduplicated form of G71 (ἄγω);</source>
G5468.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a bit-leader</def>, i.e. to curb (figuratively)</meaning>

G5469.meaning: <meaning><def>a curb or head-stall</def> (as curbing the spirit)</meaning>

G5474.source: <source>neuter of a compound of G5475 (χαλκός) and G3030 (λίβανος) (in the implied mean of whiteness or brilliancy);</source>
G5474.meaning: <meaning><def>burnished copper</def>, an alloy of copper (or gold) and silver having a brilliant lustre</meaning>

G5475.source: <source>perhaps from G5465 (χαλάω) through the idea of hollowing out as a vessel (this metal being chiefly used for that purpose);</source>
G5475.meaning: <meaning><def>copper</def> (the substance, or some implement or coin made of it)</meaning>

G5476.source: <source>adverb perhaps from the base of G5490 (χάσμα) through the idea of a fissure in the soil;</source>

G5477.meaning: <meaning><def>Chanaan</def> (i.e. Kenaan), the early name of Palestine</meaning>

G5478.meaning: <meaning><def>a Chanaanæan</def> (i.e. Kenaanite), or native of gentile Palestine</meaning>

G5480.meaning: <meaning><def>a scratch or etching</def>, i.e. stamp (as a badge of servitude), or scupltured figure (statue)</meaning>

G5481.meaning: <meaning><def>a graver</def> (the tool or the person), i.e. (by implication) engraving (("character"), the figure stamped, i.e. an exact copy or (figuratively) representation)</meaning>

G5482.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to G1125</def> (γράφω) through the idea of scratching); a stake, i.e. (by implication) a palisade or rampart (military mound for circumvallation in a siege)</meaning>

G5483.meaning: <meaning><def>to grant as a favor</def>, i.e. gratuitously, in kindness, pardon or rescue</meaning>

G5484.meaning: <meaning><def>through favor of</def>, i.e. on account of</meaning>
G5484.usage: <usage>be-(for) cause of, for sake of, +…fore, X reproachfully.</usage>

G5485.meaning: <meaning><def>graciousness</def> (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)</meaning>
G5485.usage: <usage>acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy).</usage>

G5486.meaning: <meaning><def>a</def> (divine) gratuity, i.e. deliverance (from danger or passion); (specially), a (spiritual) endowment, i.e. (subjectively) religious qualification, or (objectively) miraculous faculty</meaning>

G5487.meaning: <meaning><def>to grace</def>, i.e. indue with special honor</meaning>

G5489.meaning: <meaning><def>a sheet</def> ("chart") of writing-material (as to be scribbled over)</meaning>

G5490.source: <source>from a form of an obsolete primary (to "gape" or "yawn");</source>

G5491.source: <source>from a form of the same as G5490 (χάσμα);</source>
G5491.meaning: <meaning><def>a lip</def> (as a pouring place); figuratively, a margin (of water)</meaning>

G5492.meaning: <meaning><def>to storm</def>, i.e. (passively) to labor under a gale</meaning>

G5493.source: <source>from the base of G5494 (χειμών) and G4482 (ῥέω);</source>

G5494.meaning: <meaning><def>akin to the base of 5490 through the idea of a channel)</def>, meaning a storm (as pouring rain); by implication, the rainy season, i.e. winter</meaning>

G5495.source: <source>perhaps from the base of G5494 (χειμών) in the sense of its congener the base of G5490 (χάσμα) (through the idea of hollowness for grasping);</source>
G5495.meaning: <meaning><def>the hand</def> (literally or figuratively (power); especially (by Hebraism) a means or instrument)</meaning>

G5496.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a hand-leader</def>, i.e. to guide (a blind person)</meaning>

G5497.source: <source>from G5495 (χείρ) and a reduplicated form of G71 (ἄγω);</source>
G5497.meaning: <meaning><def>a hand-leader</def>, i.e. personal conductor (of a blind person)</meaning>

G5498.source: <source>neuter of a compound of G5495 (χείρ) and G1125 (γράφω);</source>
G5498.meaning: <meaning><def>something hand-written</def> ("chirograph"), i.e. a manuscript (specially, a legal document or bond (figuratively))</meaning>

G5499.source: <source>from G5495 (χείρ) and a derivative of G4160 (ποιέω);</source>

G5500.source: <source>from a comparative of G5495 (χείρ) and (to stretch);</source>
G5500.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a hand-reacher or voter</def> (by raising the hand), i.e. (generally) to select or appoint</meaning>

G5501.meaning: <meaning><def>from an obsolete equivalent</def> (of uncertain derivation); more evil or aggravated (physically, mentally or morally)</meaning>

G5503.source: <source>feminine of a presumed derivative apparently from the base of G5490 (χάσμα) through the idea of deficiency;</source>
G5503.meaning: <meaning><def>a widow</def> (as lacking a husband), literally or figuratively</meaning>

G5504.meaning: <meaning><def>"yesterday"</def>; by extension, in time past or hitherto</meaning>

G5506.meaning: <meaning><def>the commander of a thousand soldiers</def> ("chiliarch"; i.e. colonel</meaning>

G5510.source: <source>perhaps akin to the base of G5490 (χάσμα) (G5465 (χαλάω)) or G5494 (χειμών) (as descending or empty);</source>

G5512.meaning: <meaning><def>to throw out the lip</def>, i.e. jeer at</meaning>

G5516.source: <source>the 22nd, 14th and an obsolete letter (G4742 (στίγμα) as a cross) of the Greek alphabet (intermediate between the 5th and 6th), used as numbers; denoting respectively 600, 60 and 6;</source>

G5521.source: <source>feminine of an equivalent perhaps akin to the same as 5514 (from the greenish hue);</source>
G5521.meaning: <meaning><def>"gall" or bile</def>, i.e. (by analogy) poison or an anodyne (wormwood, poppy, etc.)</meaning>

G5522.meaning: <meaning><def>a heap</def> (as poured out), i.e. rubbish; loose dirt</meaning>

G5524.source: <source>from a compound of G5525 (χορός) and G71 (ἄγω);</source>
G5524.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a dance-leader</def>, i.e. (generally) to furnish</meaning>

G5526.meaning: <meaning><def>to fodder</def>, i.e. (generally) to gorge (supply food in abundance)</meaning>

G5528.meaning: <meaning><def>a "court" or "garden"</def>, i.e. (by implication, of pasture) herbage or vegetation</meaning>

G5530.source: <source>middle voice of a primary verb (perhaps rather from G5495 (χείρ), to handle);</source>
G5530.meaning: <meaning><def>to furnish what is needed</def>; (give an oracle, "graze" (touch slightly), light upon, etc.), i.e. (by implication) to employ or (by extension) to act towards one in a given manner</meaning>

G5531.source: <source>probably the same as the base of G5530 (χράομαι);</source>

G5532.source: <source>from the base of G5530 (χράομαι) or G5534 (χρή);</source>
G5532.meaning: <meaning><def>employment</def>, i.e. an affair; also (by implication) occasion, demand, requirement or destitution</meaning>

G5533.source: <source>from a derivative of G5531 (χράω) and G3781 (ὀφειλέτης);</source>

G5534.source: <source>third person singular of the same as G5530 (χράομαι) or G5531 (χράω) used impersonally;</source>

G5535.meaning: <meaning><def>to make</def> (i.e. have) necessity, i.e. be in want of</meaning>

G5536.meaning: <meaning><def>something useful or needed</def>, i.e. wealth, price</meaning>

G5537.meaning: <meaning><def>to utter an oracle</def> (compare the original sense of G5530 (χράομαι)), i.e. divinely intimate; by implication, (compare the secular sense of G5532 (χρεία)) to constitute a firm for business, i.e. (generally) bear as a title</meaning>
G5537.usage: <usage>be called, be admonished (warned) of God, reveal, speak.</usage>

G5540.meaning: <meaning><def>employment</def>, i.e. (specially), sexual intercourse (as an occupation of the body)</meaning>

G5541.meaning: <meaning><def>to show oneself useful</def>, i.e. act benevolently</meaning>

G5542.source: <source>from a compound of G5543 (χρηστός) and G3004 (λέγω);</source>

G5543.meaning: <meaning><def>employed</def>, i.e. (by implication) useful (in manner or morals)</meaning>

G5544.meaning: <meaning><def>usefulness</def>, i.e. morally, excellence (in character or demeanor)</meaning>

G5545.meaning: <meaning><def>an unguent or smearing</def>, i.e. (figuratively) the special endowment ("chrism") of the Holy Spirit</meaning>

G5547.meaning: <meaning><def>anointed</def>, i.e. the Messiah, an epithet of Jesus</meaning>

G5548.source: <source>probably akin to G5530 (χράομαι) through the idea of contact;</source>
G5548.meaning: <meaning><def>to smear or rub with oil</def>, i.e. (by implication) to consecrate to an office or religious service</meaning>

G5550.meaning: <meaning><def>a space of time</def> (in general, and thus properly distinguished from G2540 (καιρός), which designates a fixed or special occasion; and from G165 (αἰών), which denotes a particular period) or interval; by extension, an individual opportunity; by implication, delay</meaning>
G5550.usage: <usage>+ years old, season, space, (X often-)time(-s), (a) while.</usage>

G5551.source: <source>from a presumed compound of G5550 (χρόνος) and the base of G5147 (τρίβος);</source>
G5551.meaning: <meaning><def>to be a time-wearer</def>, i.e. to procrastinate (linger)</meaning>

G5553.meaning: <meaning><def>a golden article</def>, i.e. gold plating, ornament, or coin</meaning>

G5554.meaning: <meaning><def>gold-ringed</def>, i.e. wearing a golden finger-ring or similar jewelry</meaning>

G5557.source: <source>perhaps from the base of G5530 (χράομαι) (through the idea of the utility of the metal);</source>
G5557.meaning: <meaning><def>gold</def>; by extension, a golden article, as an ornament or coin</meaning>

G5558.meaning: <meaning><def>to gild</def>, i.e. bespangle with golden ornaments</meaning>

G5559.source: <source>probably akin to the base of G5530 (χράομαι) through the idea of handling;</source>

G5561.source: <source>feminine of a derivative of the base of G5490 (χάσμα) through the idea of empty expanse;</source>
G5561.meaning: <meaning><def>room</def>, i.e. a space of territory (more or less extensive; often including its inhabitants)</meaning>

G5562.meaning: <meaning><def>to be in</def> (give) space, i.e. (intransitively) to pass, enter, or (transitively) to hold, admit (literally or figuratively)</meaning>
G5562.usage: <usage>come, contain, go, have place, (can, be room to) receive.</usage>

G5563.meaning: <meaning><def>to place room between</def>, i.e. part; reflexively, to go away</meaning>

G5564.usage: <usage>field, land, parcel of ground, place, possession.</usage>

G5565.meaning: <meaning><def>at a space</def>, i.e. separately or apart from (often as preposition)</meaning>

G5567.source: <source>probably strengthened from (to rub or touch the surface;</source>
G5567.meaning: <meaning><def>compare G5597</def> (ψώχω)); to twitch or twang, i.e. to play on a stringed instrument (celebrate the divine worship with music and accompanying odes)</meaning>

G5568.meaning: <meaning><def>a set piece of music</def>, i.e. a sacred ode (accompanied with the voice, harp or other instrument; a "psalm"); collectively, the book of the Psalms</meaning>

G5572.meaning: <meaning><def>a spurious teacher</def>, i.e. propagator of erroneous Christian doctrine</meaning>

G5574.source: <source>middle voice of an apparently primary verb;</source>
G5574.meaning: <meaning><def>to utter an untruth or attempt to deceive by falsehood</def></meaning>

G5575.source: <source>from G5571 (ψευδής) and a kindred form of G3144 (μάρτυς);</source>
G5575.meaning: <meaning><def>a spurious witness</def>, i.e. bearer of untrue testimony</meaning>

G5576.meaning: <meaning><def>to be an untrue testifier</def>, i.e. offer falsehood in evidence</meaning>

G5578.meaning: <meaning><def>a spurious prophet</def>, i.e. pretended foreteller or religious impostor</meaning>

G5584.meaning: <meaning><def>to manipulate</def>, i.e. verify by contact; figuratively, to search for</meaning>

G5585.meaning: <meaning><def>to use pebbles in enumeration</def>, i.e. (generally) to compute</meaning>

G5586.meaning: <meaning><def>a pebble</def> (as worn smooth by handling), i.e. (by implication, of use as a counter or ballot) a verdict (of acquittal) or ticket (of admission); a vote</meaning>

G5587.meaning: <meaning><def>by implication</def>, a slander; probably akin to G5574 (ψεύδομαι)); whispering, i.e. secret detraction</meaning>

G5589.source: <source>diminutive from a derivative of the base of G5567 (ψάλλω) (meaning a crumb);</source>

G5590.meaning: <meaning><def>breath</def>, i.e. (by implication) spirit, abstractly or concretely (the animal sentient principle only; thus distinguished on the one hand from G4151 (πνεῦμα), which is the rational and immortal soul; and on the other from G2222 (ζωή), which is mere vitality, even of plants: these terms thus exactly correspond respectively to the Hebrew H05315, H07307 and H02416)</meaning>

G5591.meaning: <meaning><def>sensitive</def>, i.e. animate (in distinction on the one hand from G4152 (πνευματικός), which is the higher or renovated nature; and on the other from G5446 (φυσικός), which is the lower or bestial nature)</meaning>

G5594.meaning: <meaning><def>to breathe</def> (voluntarily but gently, thus differing on the one hand from G4154 (πνέω), which denotes properly a forcible respiration; and on the other from the base of G109 (ἀήρ), which refers properly to an inanimate breeze), i.e. (by implication, of reduction of temperature by evaporation) to chill (figuratively)</meaning>

G5595.meaning: <meaning><def>to supply with bits</def>, i.e. (generally) to nourish</meaning>

G5596.source: <source>diminutive from a derivative of the base of G5597 (ψώχω);</source>
G5596.meaning: <meaning><def>a crumb or morsel</def> (as if rubbed off), i.e. a mouthful</meaning>

G5597.source: <source>prolongation from the same base as G5567 (ψάλλω);</source>
G5597.meaning: <meaning><def>to triturate</def>, i.e. (by analogy) to rub out (kernels from husks with the fingers or hand)</meaning>

G5598.meaning: <meaning><def>the last letter of the Greek alphabet</def>, i.e. (figuratively) the finality</meaning>

G5599.meaning: <meaning><def>as a sign of the vocative case</def>, O; as a note of exclamation, oh</meaning>

G5600.meaning: <meaning><def>(may</def>, might, can, could, would, should, must, etc.; also with G1487 (εἰ) and its comparative, as well as with other particles) be</meaning>
G5600.usage: <usage>+ appear, are, (may, might, should) be, X have, is, + pass the flower of her age, should stand, were.</usage>

G5602.meaning: <meaning><def>in this same spot</def>, i.e. here or hither</meaning>

G5603.meaning: <meaning><def>a chant or "ode"</def> (the general term for any words sung; while G5215 (ὕμνος) denotes especially a religious metrical composition, and G5568 (ψαλμός) still more specially, a Hebrew cantillation)</meaning>

G5604.meaning: <meaning><def>a pang or throe</def>, especially of childbirth</meaning>

G5605.meaning: <meaning><def>to experience the pains of parturition</def> (literally or figuratively)</meaning>

G5606.meaning: <meaning><def>the shoulder</def> (as that on which burdens are borne)</meaning>

G5608.source: <source>middle voice from an apparently primary (a sum or price);</source>

G5610.usage: <usage>day, hour, instant, season, X short, (even-)tide, (high) time.</usage>

G5611.meaning: <meaning><def>belonging to the right hour or season</def> (timely), i.e. (by implication) flourishing (beauteous (figuratively))</meaning>

G5612.source: <source>middle voice of an apparently primary verb;</source>

G5613.meaning: <meaning><def>which how</def>, i.e. in that manner (very variously used, as follows)</meaning>
G5613.usage: <usage>about, after (that), (according) as (it had been, it were), as soon (as), even as (like), for, how (greatly), like (as, unto), since, so (that), that, to wit, unto, when(-soever), while, X with all speed.</usage>

G5614.meaning: <meaning><def>oh save!</def>; hosanna (i.e. hoshia-na), an exclamation of adoration</meaning>

G5615.source: <source>from G5613 (ὡς) and an adverb from G846 (αὐτός);</source>
G5615.meaning: <meaning><def>as thus</def>, i.e. in the same way</meaning>
G5615.usage: <usage>even so, likewise, after the same (in like) manner.</usage>

G5616.usage: <usage>about, as (it had been, it were), like (as).</usage>

G5619.meaning: <meaning><def>just as if</def>, i.e. as it were</meaning>

G5620.meaning: <meaning><def>so too</def>, i.e. thus therefore (in various relations of consecution, as follow)</meaning>
G5620.usage: <usage>(insomuch) as, so that (then), (insomuch) that, therefore, to, wherefore.</usage>

G5621.meaning: <meaning><def>an earlet</def>, i.e. one of the ears, or perhaps the lobe of the ear</meaning>

G5622.source: <source>from a derivative of the base of G5624 (ὠφέλιμος);</source>
